info.title: Snugg Pro API
info.description:
Access Snugg Pro programmatically through our API.
Read this article for authentication information

get.name: account
get.description: A single Snugg Pro user by account ID

get.name: jobAccount
get.description: Retrieves the information of the user account that the job is assigned to

get.name: accounts
get.description:
This endpoint is DEPRECATED. Use one of "/companies/:companyId/accounts" or "/programs/:programId/accounts" instead.
Company API:
Returns a list of all active Snugg Pro users for a company. These are not your customers, they are people who can log into Snugg Pro.
Program API:
These are your program-level accounts.

get.name: job
get.description: Address and metadata about a job

parameters.name: jobId
parameters.description: Job Id

parameters.name: serviceTime
parameters.description: Time at which the appointment will take place. Format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the Snugg Pro user who owns this job.

parameters.name: companyId
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the Snugg Pro company who owns the job.

parameters.name: note
parameters.description: Note to assignee or assigned user.

parameters.name: householdIncomeTier
parameters.description: Household income tier for the job.

get.name: allJobData
get.description: Excludes Workflow ticket data. This endpoint iterates over all the other job GET endpoints to return all data about a given job in a single response. The recommended method is to hit other job endpoints individually to retrieve only the data you need. This endpoint is not very nimble and should be used minimally.

get.name: attics
get.description: All available attics for a job

get.name: basedata
get.description: All the simple data about the home - Not Base and Improved

parameters.name: yearBuilt
parameters.description: Enter the year the home or building was constructed. If there was a major addition made to the home, the "Year built" should be entered as the year the majority of the area that is heated and air conditioned was built.

parameters.name: conditionedArea
parameters.description: Enter the total area of the home that is heated or air conditioned. This may be different from the typical measure of the size of the home, especially if you have a heated garage or similar area of the home that receives heating or air conditioning. Make sure to include any space of the home that has heating or air conditioning, including conditioned basements (unless you mark the next field "Area Includes Basement" as No).

parameters.name: averageWallHeight
parameters.description:
Enter the average floor to ceiling wall height (internal measurement). This is used to calculate the volume of the home as well as the wall surface area.  
  
**Please note:** When we calculate the volume of the house, we add an additional foot of height for the basement rim joists (if there is a basement), and an additional foot of height for every story above one (if there are any).

parameters.name: houseLength
parameters.description:
Enter the length of the longest side of the smallest rectangle into which the home's footprint will fit.  
  
**Please note:** This is used only to calculate the surface area of the exterior walls, and has nothing to do with the conditioned square footage, volume, or any other calculations.  
  
This should give a fairly precise estimate of wall surface area in all circumstances except when the house is shaped like a U. If the home is shaped like a U, you'll need to override the total wall sqft in the Wall Details section with the additional surface area. See the knowledge base article on this for more information.

parameters.name: houseWidth
parameters.description:
Enter the width of the shortest side of the smallest rectangle into which the home's footprint will fit.  
  
**Please note:** This is used only to calculate the surface area of the exterior walls, and has nothing to do with the conditioned square footage, volume, or any other calculations.  
  
This should give a fairly precise estimate of wall surface area in all circumstances except when the house is shaped like a U. If the home is shaped like a U, you'll need to override the total wall sqft in the Wall Details section with the additional surface area. See the knowledge base article on this for more information.

parameters.name: floorsAboveGrade
parameters.description: Select the number of stories for the living area of this home, not counting any basement or walkout as a story. If this home is in a multi-story, multi-family complex, report only those stories in the living area of this particular home, which could likely be less than the total number of stories of the building.

parameters.name: numberOfOccupants
parameters.description:
Enter the number of people that live full-time in the home. If there are people that live in the home only part of the year (e.g., a student away at college or an adult that travels for business a significant amount each year) then for purposes of this audit count that individual as only 1/2 (0.5) of a person.  
  
This field directly affects human internal gains. If you don't set the bedrooms, then the occupants are used to estimate the bedrooms. Only the bedrooms field impact appliance usage levels. Occupants also impact the ASHRAE 62.2-2013 minimum ventilation.

parameters.name: typeOfHome
parameters.description:
Select the type of home that most closely resembles this home. Single-family Attached would be a duplex or a townhome with shared walls.  
  
**Please note:** that only multi-family units with independently metered fuel and electricity can be modeled with this software.

parameters.name: frontOfBuildingOrientation
parameters.description: Select the closest direction that the Length of the house as designated above faces (either of the opposite directions is fine). If the home is square, then choose the direction the main entry door of the home faces.

parameters.name: tuckUnderGarage
parameters.description:
Is there any conditioned living space over an unconditioned garage, cantilever, or frame floor that might need floor insulation?  
  
This data is used for the "Insulate Floors" recommendation. For cantilevers, adjust the total sqft on the refine screen or the details section.

parameters.name: garageSize
parameters.description:
Enter the number of cars that could fit in the the parking area of the garage. The software assumes an extra 100 sqft of storage and other space and each car takes up 200 sqft of space.  
  
This data is used for the "Insulate Floors" recommendation. For cantilevers, adjust the total sqft on the refine screen or the details section.

parameters.name: programmableThermostatInstalled
parameters.description: Is a programmable (setback) thermostat installed in the house? If it is installed, but not used, still check this box and adjust the thermostat settings accordingly. This button determines if a cost is added to the Programmable Thermostat recommendation. If you check Yes, then no cost will be added, but the software will still recommend ideal setpoints for the home for energy savings. If you check No, then a cost will be added to the recommendation and the ideal setpoints will also be shown in the recommendation.

parameters.name: heatingSetpointHigh
parameters.description:
Enter the heating thermostat temperature setting (°F) that is used most often when the home is occupied and used by people for normal daily activities. This is often the setting used when people are at home in the mornings before going to school or work and again when they return home from school and work in the afternoon or evening before they go to sleep for the night.  
  
**Note:** The goal of this is to determine the average temperature of the home for this time period and situation (cooling or heating). If the heating system just can't keep up and never meets the setpoint on cold days or similar for the cooling system, then adjust the setpoint to closer resemble the actual temperature of the home. Since this is hard to determine, you may enter a range of temperatures for each of the setpoints separated by a dash (no spaces). For example, you can set the Low temp setpoint for heating at 60-65 and the High temp setpoint at 68-71.

parameters.name: thermostatManufacturer
parameters.description: Enter the manufacturer of the Thermostat.

parameters.name: thermostatManufacturerImproved
parameters.description: Enter the manufacturer of the Thermostat.

parameters.name: thermostatModel
parameters.description: Enter the model of the Thermostat.

parameters.name: thermostatModelImproved
parameters.description: Enter the model of the Thermostat.

parameters.name: thermostatSerialNumber
parameters.description: Enter the serial number of the Thermostat.

parameters.name: thermostatSerialNumberImproved
parameters.description: Enter the serial number of the Thermostat.

parameters.name: totalNumberOfLightBulbs
parameters.description: Count the number of light bulbs in the home. This count assumes an average of 60 watts for each bulb. Remember to look in closets and other hidden areas for lights as well. In the Details section the Base and Improved numbers are calculated as a total of the number of bulbs for each side.

parameters.name: totalNumberOfLightBulbsImproved
parameters.description: Count the number of light bulbs in the home. This count assumes an average of 60 watts for each bulb. Remember to look in closets and other hidden areas for lights as well. In the Details section the Base and Improved numbers are calculated as a total of the number of bulbs for each side.

parameters.name: sharedWallsNorth
parameters.description: Enter the percent of this wall that is shared with another unit (assumed an adiabatic wall).

parameters.name: percentOfCeilingsShared
parameters.description: Select the % of heated or conditioned space (from a seperatly metered apartment or unit) above your apartment / condominium / single-family attached home? If this is a single family detached home, leave this blank or at 0.

parameters.name: percentOfFloorsShared
parameters.description: Select the % of heated or conditioned space (from a separately metered apartment or unit) below your apartment / condominium / single-family attached home? If this is a single family detached home, leave this blank or at 0.

parameters.name: foundationBasement
parameters.description: Select the percentage of foundation that is **basement**, **crawlspace** or **slab on grade**. Total of all three foundation types cannot exceed 100%.

parameters.name: foundationAboveGradeHeight
parameters.description: For the parts of the basement or crawl space that are above grade (above ground), enter the average height (in feet) of this exposed foundation for the entire perimeter of the home.

parameters.name: basementWallInsulation
enum: Unfinished frame wall with fiberglass batts
parameters.description: Select the type of materials used, if any, on the basement walls of this home. If more than one type of material is used on the basement walls, select the type of material that is used on the most wall area of the basement. If the basement walls have insulation installed on the exterior of the walls, select the option "Finished walls with insulation".

parameters.name: basementWallInsulationImproved
enum: Unfinished frame wall with fiberglass batts
parameters.description: Select the type of materials used, if any, on the basement walls of this home. If more than one type of material is used on the basement walls, select the type of material that is used on the most wall area of the basement. If the basement walls have insulation installed on the exterior of the walls, select the option "Finished walls with insulation".

parameters.name: crawlspaceInsulation
enum: Crawlspace has insulation installed on the exterior wall area
Crawlspace has insulation installed under only the living space floor
parameters.description: What you type here will show up in the details page for "Insulate Crawl Space" on the homeowner report.

parameters.name: skylightArea
parameters.description: Enter the area of any skylights (in square feet) or roof windows that are installed in the home within the heated or air conditioned space of the home.

parameters.name: blowerDoorReading
parameters.description:
The baseline airflow, in cubic feet per minute, through leaks in the building when there is a pressure difference between the building and ambient of 50 Pascals. This is the result of your blower door test if you perform one. If you do not enter a number here, the software will determine an appropriate number based on the age and size of the home.  
  
On the improved side, the default recommendation is a 25% reduction in the air leakage. You may of course override this to whatever number you feel you'll be able to achieve.

parameters.name: blowerDoorReadingImproved
parameters.description:
The baseline airflow, in cubic feet per minute, through leaks in the building when there is a pressure difference between the building and ambient of 50 Pascals. This is the result of your blower door test if you perform one. If you do not enter a number here, the software will determine an appropriate number based on the age and size of the home.  
  
On the improved side, the default recommendation is a 25% reduction in the air leakage. You may of course override this to whatever number you feel you'll be able to achieve.

parameters.name: unmodeledBlowerDoorReading
parameters.description:
The baseline unmodeled airflow, in cubic feet per minute, through leaks in the building when there is a pressure difference between the building and ambient of 50 Pascals. This is the result of your blower door test if you perform one. If you do not enter a number here, the software will determine an appropriate number based on the age and size of the home.  
  
On the improved side, the default recommendation is a 25% reduction in the air leakage. You may of course override this to whatever number you feel you'll be able to achieve.

parameters.name: unmodeledBlowerDoorReadingImproved
parameters.description:
The baseline unmodeled airflow, in cubic feet per minute, through leaks in the building when there is a pressure difference between the building and ambient of 50 Pascals. This is the result of your blower door test if you perform one. If you do not enter a number here, the software will determine an appropriate number based on the age and size of the home.  
  
On the improved side, the default recommendation is a 25% reduction in the air leakage. You may of course override this to whatever number you feel you'll be able to achieve.

parameters.name: equivalentNach
parameters.description: NACH is the number of times in one hour the entire volume of air inside the building leaks to the outside naturally. It is calculated from the Air Infiltration number in CFM50, the number of stories, the wind zone and type of shielding the house has.

parameters.name: equivalentNachImproved
parameters.description: NACH is the number of times in one hour the entire volume of air inside the building leaks to the outside naturally. It is calculated from the Air Infiltration number in CFM50, the number of stories, the wind zone and type of shielding the house has.

parameters.name: conditionedAirVolume
parameters.description: The conditioned air volume is calculated from the Conditioned Area and the Average Wall Height. This is really available for informational purposes and in most cases should not be edited.

parameters.name: effectiveLeakageArea
parameters.description: Effective (or Equivalent) Leakage Area in square inches. This is the area of a theoretical hole (with rounded edges) in the building envelope that would produce a leakage equivalent to that produced by the actual building at 4 Pascals of pressure.

parameters.name: effectiveLeakageAreaImproved
parameters.description: Effective (or Equivalent) Leakage Area in square inches. This is the area of a theoretical hole (with rounded edges) in the building envelope that would produce a leakage equivalent to that produced by the actual building at 4 Pascals of pressure.

parameters.name: equivalentAch50
parameters.description: ACH50 is the number of times in one hour the entire volume of air inside the building leaks to the outside when depressurized to 50 Pascals. This number is automatically calculated based on the house volume and the Air Infiltration number in CFM50.

parameters.name: equivalentAch50Improved
parameters.description: ACH50 is the number of times in one hour the entire volume of air inside the building leaks to the outside when depressurized to 50 Pascals. This number is automatically calculated based on the house volume and the Air Infiltration number in CFM50.

parameters.name: modeledBasementWallArea
parameters.description: This is the total wall area along the exposed perimeter (i.e. wall area eligible for insulation improvements). Warning: Changing the wall area will not effect the model. Wall area is only used in calculating improvement costs.

parameters.name: basementCavityInsulation
parameters.description:
Enter the total R-value of insulation installed (BASE) or to be installed (IMPROVED) in the framed cavity of the basement walls. If there is no framing, use Continuous Insulation below to specify insulation types such as fiberglass drape or rigid insulation.  
  
**Note:**  
Edit Base fields in the Details section only if you're confident of the actual value that is entered. The modeling engine utilizes utility data and input form data to determine the "effective" values on the Base side.  
  
Edit Improved fields in the Details section with the details of what you recommend be installed in the house. These values are only used if the item is Recommended. Default values are supplied by the modeling engine, but can be overridden.

parameters.name: basementCavityInsulationImproved
parameters.description:
Enter the total R-value of insulation installed (BASE) or to be installed (IMPROVED) in the framed cavity of the basement walls. If there is no framing, use Continuous Insulation below to specify insulation types such as fiberglass drape or rigid insulation.  
  
**Note:**  
Edit Base fields in the Details section only if you're confident of the actual value that is entered. The modeling engine utilizes utility data and input form data to determine the "effective" values on the Base side.  
  
Edit Improved fields in the Details section with the details of what you recommend be installed in the house. These values are only used if the item is Recommended. Default values are supplied by the modeling engine, but can be overridden.

parameters.name: basementContinuousInsulation
parameters.description:
Enter the total R-value of insulation installed (BASE) or to be installed (IMPROVED) in on the basement walls such as a fiberglass drape or rigid insulation. If the basement walls are framed out, then specify the R-value in the Cavity Insulation field above.  
  
**Note:**  
Edit Base fields in the Details section only if you're confident of the actual value that is entered. The modeling engine utilizes utility data and input form data to determine the "effective" values on the Base side.  
  
Edit Improved fields in the Details section with the details of what you recommend be installed in the house. These values are only used if the item is Recommended. Default values are supplied by the modeling engine, but can be overridden.

parameters.name: basementContinuousInsulationImproved
parameters.description:
Enter the total R-value of insulation installed (BASE) or to be installed (IMPROVED) in on the basement walls such as a fiberglass drape or rigid insulation. If the basement walls are framed out, then specify the R-value in the Cavity Insulation field above.  
  
**Note:**  
Edit Base fields in the Details section only if you're confident of the actual value that is entered. The modeling engine utilizes utility data and input form data to determine the "effective" values on the Base side.  
  
Edit Improved fields in the Details section with the details of what you recommend be installed in the house. These values are only used if the item is Recommended. Default values are supplied by the modeling engine, but can be overridden.

parameters.name: basementWallAssemblyRValue
parameters.description: This is the total assembly R-value for the entire basement wall system. We take the data from the Cavity and Continuous R-value fields and add factors for wood framing, drywall, air film, etc. to calculate the overall assembly R-value. This field is not editable since it is calculated from the other fields.

parameters.name: basementWallAssemblyRValueImproved
parameters.description: This is the total assembly R-value for the entire basement wall system. We take the data from the Cavity and Continuous R-value fields and add factors for wood framing, drywall, air film, etc. to calculate the overall assembly R-value. This field is not editable since it is calculated from the other fields.

parameters.name: floorCavityInsulation
parameters.description:
Enter the total R-value of insulation installed (BASE) or to be installed (IMPROVED) in the Floor Cavity.  
  
**Note:**  
Edit Base fields in the Details section only if you're confident of the actual value that is entered. The modeling engine utilizes utility data and input form data to determine the "effective" values on the Base side.  
  
Edit Improved fields in the Details section with the details of what you recommend be installed in the house. These values are only used if the item is Recommended. Default values are supplied by the modeling engine, but can be overridden.

parameters.name: floorCavityInsulationImproved
parameters.description:
Enter the total R-value of insulation installed (BASE) or to be installed (IMPROVED) in the Floor Cavity.  
  
**Note:**  
Edit Base fields in the Details section only if you're confident of the actual value that is entered. The modeling engine utilizes utility data and input form data to determine the "effective" values on the Base side.  
  
Edit Improved fields in the Details section with the details of what you recommend be installed in the house. These values are only used if the item is Recommended. Default values are supplied by the modeling engine, but can be overridden.

parameters.name: floorContinuousInsulation
parameters.description:
Enter the total R-value of continuous insulation installed or to be installed under the Frame Floor. Continuous insulation is any insulation like spray foam or rigid foam that is continuous and consistent in R-value across studs, joists, or any framing member.  
  
**Note:**  
Edit Base fields in the Details section only if you're confident of the actual value that is entered. The modeling engine utilizes utility data and input form data to determine the "effective" values on the Base side.  
  
Edit Improved fields in the Details section with the details of what you recommend be installed in the house. These values are only used if the item is Recommended. Default values are supplied by the modeling engine, but can be overridden.

parameters.name: floorContinuousInsulationImproved
parameters.description:
Enter the total R-value of continuous insulation installed or to be installed under the Frame Floor. Continuous insulation is any insulation like spray foam or rigid foam that is continuous and consistent in R-value across studs, joists, or any framing member.  
  
**Note:**  
Edit Base fields in the Details section only if you're confident of the actual value that is entered. The modeling engine utilizes utility data and input form data to determine the "effective" values on the Base side.  
  
Edit Improved fields in the Details section with the details of what you recommend be installed in the house. These values are only used if the item is Recommended. Default values are supplied by the modeling engine, but can be overridden.

parameters.name: floorAssemblyRValue
parameters.description: This is the total assembly R-value for the entire frame floor system. We take the data from the Cavity and Continuous R-value fields and add factors for wood framing, drywall, air film, sheathing, etc. to calculate the overall assembly R-value. This field is not editable since it is calculated from the other fields.

parameters.name: floorAssemblyRValueImproved
parameters.description: This is the total assembly R-value for the entire frame floor system. We take the data from the Cavity and Continuous R-value fields and add factors for wood framing, drywall, air film, sheathing, etc. to calculate the overall assembly R-value. This field is not editable since it is calculated from the other fields.

parameters.name: modeledFloorArea
parameters.description:
This field will be automatically calculated based on the data you entered in the input form for Tuck Under Garage and the associated Garage Size. This field can also be used to identify cantilevers over other unconditioned space.  
  
Enter the area of frame floors that serve as a thermal boundary to the conditioned space, excluding those over foundation crawl spaces or basements.

parameters.name: sharedWallsEast
parameters.description: Enter the percent of this wall that is shared with another unit (assumed an adiabatic wall).

parameters.name: sharedWallsSouth
parameters.description: Enter the percent of this wall that is shared with another unit (assumed an adiabatic wall).

parameters.name: sharedWallsWest
parameters.description: Enter the percent of this wall that is shared with another unit (assumed an adiabatic wall).

parameters.name: foundationCrawlspace
parameters.description: Select the percentage of foundation that is **basement**, **crawlspace** or **slab on grade**. Total of all three foundation types cannot exceed 100%.

parameters.name: foundationSlab
parameters.description: Select the percentage of foundation that is **basement**, **crawlspace** or **slab on grade**. Total of all three foundation types cannot exceed 100%.

parameters.name: foundationFramefloor
parameters.description: Select the percentage of foundation that is **basement**, **crawlspace**, **slab on grade** or **frame floor**. Total of the foundation type cannot exceed 100%.

parameters.name: percentCflsOrLeds
parameters.description: Select the percentage range that most closely represents the percentage of lamps and fixtures on the home that are fitted with compact fluorescent lights (CLFs).

parameters.name: crawlCavityInsulation
parameters.description:
Enter the total R-value of insulation installed (BASE) or to be installed (IMPROVED) in the framed cavity of the crawlspace ceiling. This field is active only if you are treating the crawlspace as an unconditioned space.  
  
**Note:**  
Edit Base fields in the Details section only if you're confident of the actual value that is entered. The modeling engine utilizes utility data and input form data to determine the "effective" values on the Base side.  
  
Edit Improved fields in the Details section with the details of what you recommend be installed in the house. These values are only used if the item is Recommended. Default values are supplied by the modeling engine, but can be overridden.

parameters.name: crawlCavityInsulationImproved
parameters.description:
Enter the total R-value of insulation installed (BASE) or to be installed (IMPROVED) in the framed cavity of the crawlspace ceiling. This field is active only if you are treating the crawlspace as an unconditioned space.  
  
**Note:**  
Edit Base fields in the Details section only if you're confident of the actual value that is entered. The modeling engine utilizes utility data and input form data to determine the "effective" values on the Base side.  
  
Edit Improved fields in the Details section with the details of what you recommend be installed in the house. These values are only used if the item is Recommended. Default values are supplied by the modeling engine, but can be overridden.

parameters.name: crawlWallInsulation
parameters.description:
Enter the total R-value of insulation installed (BASE) or to be installed (IMPROVED) in on the crawlspace walls such as a fiberglass drape or spray foam insulation. This field is active only if you are treating the crawlspace as a conditioned space.  
  
**Note:**  
Edit Base fields in the Details section only if you're confident of the actual value that is entered. The modeling engine utilizes utility data and input form data to determine the "effective" values on the Base side.  
  
Edit Improved fields in the Details section with the details of what you recommend be installed in the house. These values are only used if the item is Recommended. Default values are supplied by the modeling engine, but can be overridden.

**Note:**  
Edit Base fields in the Details section only if you're confident of the actual value that is entered. The modeling engine utilizes utility data and input form data to determine the "effective" values on the Base side.

parameters.name: modeledCrawlFloorArea

parameters.name: crawlspaceType
parameters.description:
Choose the type of venting and conditioning in the crawlspace. Your choice will enable or disable the Wall and Floor Cavity Insulation fields in the Crawlspace Details section depending on the appropriate situation.  
  
**Unvented - Unconditioned Crawl**: This unconditioned crawlspace scenario is where the building floor above the crawl space is the thermal boundary to the home. If there were insulation, it would be applied applied to the building floor (crawlspace ceiling). This crawlspace does not have direct vents to the outside air.  
  
**Vented - Year Round**: This is an unconditioned crawlspace where the building floor above the crawl space is the thermal boundary to the home. If there were insulation, it would be applied to the building floor (crawlspace ceiling). This crawlspace has direct vents to the outside air, and those vents remain open all year long.  
  
**Vented - Summer Only**: This is an unconditioned crawlspace where the building floor above the crawl space is the thermal boundary to the home. If there were insulation, it would be applied to the building floor (crawlspace ceiling). This crawlspace has direct vents to the outside air, and those vents remain open only during summer months.  
  
**Conditioned Crawl**: This is a conditioned crawlspace where the foundation walls and ground/slab of the crawl space are the thermal boundary. If there is insulation, it would be applied to the foundation wall and rim joists. In this scenario, there is no venting to the outside and the crawlspace floor is covered with a vapor barrier on top of the dirt floor. Building code often requires adding a jump vent to the main conditioned space in the house or ducting the furnace and/or A/C into the crawlspace as well. If the crawl does not have a jump vent or supply ducts, you will likely get a slight overestimate of energy use. The model assumes that the crawl temperature is a mixture of 20% ground temp and 80% indoor temp. Note: This field also triggers the Rim Joist options for the Crawlspace. You must set this field to Conditioned Crawl to be able to specify the insulation of the Rim Joists in the Crawlspace.

parameters.name: crawlspaceTypeImproved
parameters.description:
Choose the type of venting and conditioning in the crawlspace. Your choice will enable or disable the Wall and Floor Cavity Insulation fields in the Crawlspace Details section depending on the appropriate situation.  
  
**Unvented - Unconditioned Crawl**: This unconditioned crawlspace scenario is where the building floor above the crawl space is the thermal boundary to the home. If there were insulation, it would be applied applied to the building floor (crawlspace ceiling). This crawlspace does not have direct vents to the outside air.  
  
**Vented - Year Round**: This is an unconditioned crawlspace where the building floor above the crawl space is the thermal boundary to the home. If there were insulation, it would be applied to the building floor (crawlspace ceiling). This crawlspace has direct vents to the outside air, and those vents remain open all year long.  
  
**Vented - Summer Only**: This is an unconditioned crawlspace where the building floor above the crawl space is the thermal boundary to the home. If there were insulation, it would be applied to the building floor (crawlspace ceiling). This crawlspace has direct vents to the outside air, and those vents remain open only during summer months.  
  
**Conditioned Crawl**: This is a conditioned crawlspace where the foundation walls and ground/slab of the crawl space are the thermal boundary. If there is insulation, it would be applied to the foundation wall and rim joists. In this scenario, there is no venting to the outside and the crawlspace floor is covered with a vapor barrier on top of the dirt floor. Building code often requires adding a jump vent to the main conditioned space in the house or ducting the furnace and/or A/C into the crawlspace as well. If the crawl does not have a jump vent or supply ducts, you will likely get a slight overestimate of energy use. The model assumes that the crawl temperature is a mixture of 20% ground temp and 80% indoor temp. Note: This field also triggers the Rim Joist options for the Crawlspace. You must set this field to Conditioned Crawl to be able to specify the insulation of the Rim Joists in the Crawlspace.

parameters.name: poolPumpType
parameters.description: Select the Pump Type from Single Speed, Two Speed, or Variable Speed (or No Improvement or None). Enter the HP (or leave blank for the default) for a Single Speed or Two Speed pump, or Turnover for a variable speed pump. Wattage of variable speed pumps depends on the speed the pump is running, and the speed will depend on the turnover, so electrical usage is determined by turnover (number of times all the water in the pool circulates through the filter in a day). The Variable Speed Pump analysis includes an optimization routine that calculates the minimal electrical usage for a variable speed pump for the selected turnover. Therefore proper setup of the pump is required to achieve these results.

parameters.name: poolPumpTypeImproved
parameters.description: Select the Pump Type from Single Speed, Two Speed, or Variable Speed (or No Improvement or None). Enter the HP (or leave blank for the default) for a Single Speed or Two Speed pump, or Turnover for a variable speed pump. Wattage of variable speed pumps depends on the speed the pump is running, and the speed will depend on the turnover, so electrical usage is determined by turnover (number of times all the water in the pool circulates through the filter in a day). The Variable Speed Pump analysis includes an optimization routine that calculates the minimal electrical usage for a variable speed pump for the selected turnover. Therefore proper setup of the pump is required to achieve these results.

parameters.name: basementHeating
parameters.description:
Please describe the heating situation in the basement.  
  
**Intentional:**  

*   Basement receives heat in the same manner as the rest of the house.

**Intentional w/ continuous circulation:**  

*   Basement air is continuously exchanged with upper floors, equalizing temperatures.

**Incidental-Desired (e.g. leaky ducts):**  

*   Basement is semi-conditioned by presence of HVAC equipment.
*   Semi-conditioning is desired and measures will not be taken to eliminate it.
*   Thermal boundary for model will be located at basement walls and floor.

**None or Undesired Incidental:**  

*   Basement is not intentionally conditioned.
*   Incidental conditioning is undesired and considered wasted energy.
*   Thermal boundary for model will be the building floor above the basement.

parameters.name: basementHeatingImproved
parameters.description:
Please describe the heating situation in the basement.  
  
**Intentional:**  

*   Basement receives heat in the same manner as the rest of the house.

**Intentional w/ continuous circulation:**

**Incidental-Desired (e.g. leaky ducts):**  

*   Basement is semi-conditioned by presence of HVAC equipment.
*   Semi-conditioning is desired and measures will not be taken to eliminate it.
*   Thermal boundary for model will be located at basement walls and floor.

**None or Undesired Incidental:**  

*   Basement is not intentionally conditioned.
*   Incidental conditioning is undesired and considered wasted energy.
*   Thermal boundary for model will be the building floor above the basement.

parameters.name: basementCooling
parameters.description:
Please describe the cooling situation in the basement.  
  
**Intentional:**  

*   Basement receives cooling in the same manner as the rest of the house.

**Intentional w/ continuous circulation:**  

*   Basement air is continuously exchanged with upper floors, equalizing temperatures.

**Incidental-Desired (e.g. leaky ducts):**  

*   Basement is semi-conditioned by presence of HVAC equipment.
*   Semi-conditioning is desired and measures will not be taken to eliminate it.
*   Thermal boundary for model will be located at basement walls and floor.

**None or Undesired Incidental:**  

*   Basement is not intentionally conditioned.
*   Incidental conditioning is undesired and considered wasted energy.
*   Thermal boundary for model will be the building floor above the basement.

**Note:** If you choose "None or Undesired Incidental", then be sure to NOT include the basement square footage in the conditioned area of the home under the Building inputs.

parameters.name: crawlspaceRimJoistLength
parameters.description:
This field will be automatically calculated based on the data you entered in the input form for House Length and House Width. This is the total length of the exposed perimeter of the rim joist for the crawlspace.  
  
**Note**: This field is always displayed, regardless if the Rim Joist is actually insulated in the Crawlspace. In order for the Rim Joist to be counted in the modeling and be designated in the HPXML output, the Crawlspace Type must be set as Conditioned Crawl. Otherwise, the Rim Joist is counted as part of the Frame Floor (the crawlspace ceiling).

parameters.description:

**Note**: This field is triggered by the Crawlspace Type. In order for the Rim Joist to be counted in the modeling and be designated in the HPXML output, the Crawlspace Type must be set as Conditioned Crawl. Otherwise, the Rim Joist is counted as part of the Frame Floor (the crawlspace ceiling).

parameters.description:
Minimum infiltration in CFM50 below which ASHRAE 62.2-2013 recommends mechanical ventilation.  
  
N/A indicates that either the local ventilation inputs are not complete or the home was built after 2009. Homes built after 2009 are not eligible for infiltration credit towards whole house ventilation.

parameters.name: windowVentingUsed
parameters.description: Do the occupants make use of operable windows to cool the home when the outdoor temperature and humidity are favorable? This can eliminate cooling loads during shoulder seasons or even year-round in temperate climates. Turning this feature on will help eliminate scenarios where you previously had negative savings for some air sealing and insulation measures.

parameters.name: basementCeilingCavityInsulation
parameters.description:
Enter the total R-value of insulation installed (BASE) or to be installed (IMPROVED) in the framed cavity of the basement ceiling. This field is active only if you are treating the basement as an unconditioned space and have the Basement Heating or Basement Cooling fields set to "None or Undesired Incidnetal".  
  
**Note:**  
Edit Base fields in the Details section only if you're confident of the actual value that is entered. The modeling engine utilizes utility data and input form data to determine the "effective" values on the Base side.  
  
Edit Improved fields in the Details section with the details of what you recommend be installed in the house. These values are only used if the item is Recommended. Default values are supplied by the modeling engine, but can be overridden.

parameters.name: basementCeilingCavityInsulationImproved
parameters.description:
Enter the total R-value of insulation installed (BASE) or to be installed (IMPROVED) in the framed cavity of the basement ceiling. This field is active only if you are treating the basement as an unconditioned space and have the Basement Heating or Basement Cooling fields set to "None or Undesired Incidnetal".  
  
**Note:**  
Edit Base fields in the Details section only if you're confident of the actual value that is entered. The modeling engine utilizes utility data and input form data to determine the "effective" values on the Base side.  
  
Edit Improved fields in the Details section with the details of what you recommend be installed in the house. These values are only used if the item is Recommended. Default values are supplied by the modeling engine, but can be overridden.

parameters.name: basementCeilingContinuousInsulation
parameters.description:
Enter the total R-value of continuous insulation installed or to be installed on the basement ceiling. Continuous insulation is any insulation like spray foam or rigid foam that is continuous and consistent in R-value across studs, joists, or any framing member.  
  
**Note:**  
Edit Base fields in the Details section only if you're confident of the actual value that is entered. The modeling engine utilizes utility data and input form data to determine the "effective" values on the Base side.  
  
Edit Improved fields in the Details section with the details of what you recommend be installed in the house. These values are only used if the item is Recommended. Default values are supplied by the modeling engine, but can be overridden.

parameters.name: basementCeilingContinuousInsulationImproved
parameters.description:
Enter the total R-value of continuous insulation installed or to be installed on the basement ceiling. Continuous insulation is any insulation like spray foam or rigid foam that is continuous and consistent in R-value across studs, joists, or any framing member.  
  
**Note:**  
Edit Base fields in the Details section only if you're confident of the actual value that is entered. The modeling engine utilizes utility data and input form data to determine the "effective" values on the Base side.  
  
Edit Improved fields in the Details section with the details of what you recommend be installed in the house. These values are only used if the item is Recommended. Default values are supplied by the modeling engine, but can be overridden.

parameters.name: basementCeilingAssemblyRValue
parameters.description: This is the total assembly R-value for the entire basement ceiling system. We take the data from the Basement Ceiling Cavity R-value field and the Basement Ceiling Continuous Insulation R-value field add factors for wood framing, carpet, air film, sheathing, etc. to calculate the overall assembly R-value. This field is not editable since it is calculated from the other fields.

This field is not editable since it is calculated from the other fields.

parameters.name: atticInsulationDepth
parameters.description: Enter the depth of all installed attic or ceiling insulation. If the insulation is not evenly distributed, estimate an average depth for the area. If the insulation is evenly distributed but has different depths installed in different area, use the depth of insulation that covers the largest area. If there is no attic and instead vaulted ceilings, enter an estimate of the thickness of insulation in the vaulted cavity.

parameters.name: atticInsulationType
parameters.description: Select the type of insulation that is installed in the attic or ceiling. If more than one type of insulation is installed, select the type that covers that largest area.

parameters.name: atticInsulationTypeImproved
parameters.description: Select the type of insulation that is installed in the attic or ceiling. If more than one type of insulation is installed, select the type that covers that largest area.

parameters.name: atticModeledAtticArea
parameters.description:
This field will be automatically calculated based on the data you entered in the input form for Conditioned Area and Number of Stories.  
  
This field designates the actual interior surface area of the ceiling. On the improved side, this is the new total sqft of the attic. Reduce this number if you're switching the attic from unconditioned to a conditioned space. For instance, if the original house had 1000 sqft of attic space and you converted it to a fully conditioned attic by spray foaming the roof deck and rafters, then set the base side to 1000 sqft and the improved side to 0. Then create a vaulted ceiling that is 0 on the base side and 1118 sqft on the improved side (adding 118 sqft for a 6/12 roof pitch).

parameters.name: atticModeledAtticAreaImproved
parameters.description:
This field will be automatically calculated based on the data you entered in the input form for Conditioned Area and Number of Stories.  
  
This field designates the actual interior surface area of the ceiling. On the improved side, this is the new total sqft of the attic. Reduce this number if you're switching the attic from unconditioned to a conditioned space. For instance, if the original house had 1000 sqft of attic space and you converted it to a fully conditioned attic by spray foaming the roof deck and rafters, then set the base side to 1000 sqft and the improved side to 0. Then create a vaulted ceiling that is 0 on the base side and 1118 sqft on the improved side (adding 118 sqft for a 6/12 roof pitch).

parameters.name: atticInsulation
parameters.description:
Enter the total R-value of insulation installed (BASE) or to be installed (IMPROVED) in this Attic. Framing factors have already been taken into account, so specify the R-value of the insulation within the cavity.  
  
**Note:**  
Edit Base fields in the Details section only if you're confident of the actual value that is entered. The modeling engine utilizes utility data and input form data to determine the "effective" values on the Base side.  
  
Edit Improved fields in the Details section with the details of what you recommend be installed in the house. These values are only used if the item is Recommended. Default values are supplied by the modeling engine, but can be overridden.

parameters.name: atticInsulationImproved
parameters.description:
Enter the total R-value of insulation installed (BASE) or to be installed (IMPROVED) in this Attic. Framing factors have already been taken into account, so specify the R-value of the insulation within the cavity.  
  
**Note:**  
Edit Base fields in the Details section only if you're confident of the actual value that is entered. The modeling engine utilizes utility data and input form data to determine the "effective" values on the Base side.  
  
Edit Improved fields in the Details section with the details of what you recommend be installed in the house. These values are only used if the item is Recommended. Default values are supplied by the modeling engine, but can be overridden.

This field is not editable since it is calculated from the other fields.

parameters.name: atticRoofAbsorptance
parameters.description: Enter the Solar Absorptance of the roofing material above this attic space. Solar Absorptance is equal to 1 - Solar Reflectance. For example, if the ENERGY STAR website gives a Initial Solar Reflectance of 0.27, enter 0.73. Default numbers will be supplied automatically, so don't change these settings unless you plan to specify a cool roof as an improvement.

parameters.name: atticRoofAbsorptanceImproved
parameters.description: Enter the Solar Absorptance of the roofing material above this attic space. Solar Absorptance is equal to 1 - Solar Reflectance. For example, if the ENERGY STAR website gives a Initial Solar Reflectance of 0.27, enter 0.73. Default numbers will be supplied automatically, so don't change these settings unless you plan to specify a cool roof as an improvement.

parameters.name: atticRoofEmissivity
parameters.description: Enter the Thermal Emittance or Emissivity of the roofing material above this attic space. Default numbers will be supplied automatically, so don't change these settings unless you plan to specify a cool roof as an improvement.

parameters.name: atticRoofEmissivityImproved
parameters.description: Enter the Thermal Emittance or Emissivity of the roofing material above this attic space. Default numbers will be supplied automatically, so don't change these settings unless you plan to specify a cool roof as an improvement.

parameters.name: atticCoolRoof
parameters.description: Choose yes if you would like to specify characteristics of a cool roof (such as Whole Home Energy Solutions Qualified roof products) for either the base or improved home. Default numbers will be supplied automatically. Most contractors will not use this option unless they are actually planning to replace a roof as part of an improvement package.

parameters.name: atticCoolRoofImproved

parameters.name: dhwAge
parameters.description: Select the age in years of the hot water system that stores or delivers the domestic hot water for the home. If more than one type of system is used for hot water in the home, select the age for the one that delivers the most gallons of hot water to the home.

parameters.name: dhwLocation
parameters.description: Select the where the hot water system that stores or delivers the domestic hot water for the home is located. If more than one type of system is used for hot water in the home, select the location for the one that delivers the most gallons of hot water to the home.

parameters.name: dhwTemperatureSettings
parameters.description: Select the temperature setting for the hot water system that stores or delivers the domestic hot water for the home. If more than one type of system is used for hot water in the home, select the temperature setting for the one that delivers the most gallons of hot water to the home.

parameters.name: dhwEnergyFactor
parameters.description: Enter the rated Energy Factor of the water heater without a decimal. Sometimes, the Energy Factor of a water heater is expressed as 0.56 or 0.82. Enter it here as 56 or 82. The Min and Max values change based on the type of water heater. Energy Factors for most equipment can be found at ahridirectory.org.

parameters.name: dhwUnifiedEnergyFactor

parameters.name: hesDhwEnergyFactor
parameters.description: The efficiency of a storage water heater is indicated by its Energy Factor (EF). If you know or have a good idea what the EF is, (check the EnergyGuide label on the water heater) enter it here, otherwise enter an installed year. This number should be entered as a decimal (e.g. 0.55). The allowed range is 0.45 to 4.0. Combustion water heaters are typically between 0.5 and 0.65. Electric are typically between 0.8 and 0.95. Heat pump water heaters may be as high as 4.

parameters.name: doorArea
parameters.description:
This field will be automatically calculated based on the data you entered in the input form. You may override this number with the actual door sizes for the home.

parameters.name: freezerUsage
parameters.description:
Enter the annual energy consumption for the freezer in kWh/yr. Search our knowledge base for "Appliances Product Finder" for more information.  
  
**Note:**  
Edit this Base field in the Details section only if you're confident of the actual value that is entered. The modeling engine utilizes utility data and input form data to determine the "effective" values on the Base side.  
  
Edit Improved fields in the Details section with the details of what you recommend be installed in the house. These values are only used if the item is Recommended. Default values are supplied by the modeling engine, but can be overridden.

parameters.name: hvacSystemName
parameters.description: Give this system a name that you can use to easily differentiate it from the others like: Old Heat Pump or Basement Furnace.

parameters.name: hvacSystemEquipmentType
enum: Deep Loop Ground Source Heat Pump (shared ducts)
Open Loop Ground Source Heat Pump (shared ducts)
Shallow Loop Ground Source Heat Pump (shared ducts)
parameters.description: Select which type of HVAC system you'd like to describe. This list shows every possible type of heating or cooling system (or combination thereof) that is available in our software. Pay attention to the “shared ducts” vs. “standalone ducts” component. Note: this is very similar to the “Both” option that was available in version 4.

parameters.name: hvacUpgradeAction
enum: Keep an existing system as is
parameters.description:
Choose an action that you plan to take for this HVAC system.**Replace with a newer model:**  
Replace the existing equipment with a new version of the same thing. Eg. replace a 78 AFUE furnace with a new 98 AFUE furnace  
**Keep an existing system as is:**  
Do nothing to a system or just change the percent of total load that that old system covers in the house.  
**Remove a system permanently:**  
Rip out an entire system and replace it with something completely new, like going from a direct heater and room AC to a central heat pump. In this instance you will have a system that is described on the base side, and the improved side will be blank.  
**Install a new non-existing system:**  
A brand new system that replaces the system that was removed when you chose: Remove a system permanently.

parameters.name: hvacHeatingEnergySource
parameters.description: This is the primary fuel source. It is used to set prices and energy content.

parameters.name: hvacHeatingEnergySourceImproved
parameters.description: This is the primary fuel source. It is used to set prices and energy content.

parameters.name: hvacHeatingIsCondensing
parameters.description: Is this furnace or boiler a condensing unit? If you see PVC pipes for the combustion venting as opposed to a gravity vent, then this unit is likely a condensing unit that runs at 90 AFUE or higher. Setting this to true will set the default a calibrated number between 90 and 96 AFUE automatically.

parameters.name: hvacPercentOfTotalHeatingLoad
parameters.description: Enter the proportion of the total load for heating or cooling that this system represents. The combined heating load percentage for all heating systems specified must add up to 100%.

parameters.name: hvacPercentOfTotalHeatingLoadImproved
parameters.description: Enter the proportion of the total load for heating or cooling that this system represents. The combined heating load percentage for all heating systems specified must add up to 100%.

parameters.name: hvacHeatingCapacity
parameters.description:
Enter the output capacity of the heating equipment in (Btu/hr).  
  
**NOTE:** This has changed since v3 of Snugg Pro. v4 and later requires the number be in Btu/hr, not kBtu/hr. All v3 jobs that were migrated to v4 have been converted to the proper number by multiplying it by 1000.

parameters.name: hvacHeatingCapacityImproved
parameters.description:

parameters.description: Select the location of the duct work for this system.

parameters.name: hvacDuctInsulation
parameters.description: Select the type of insulation installed over the duct work for this system. Note: If ducts are covered wholly or partially by incidental insulation present in the cavity in which they are installed, then select the insulation type that most closely matches the average R-value of the insulation. If you know the actual duct insulation R-value, choose Measured (R-Value) and enter the data in the field below.

parameters.name: hvacDuctInsulationImproved
parameters.description: Select the type of insulation installed over the duct work for this system. parameters.name: hvacDuctLeakageValueImproved
parameters.description: If you perform a duct blaster test, please enter the tested leakage to outside at 25 Pa per RESNET Standard Section 803.7.

parameters.name: hvacPercentOfTotalCoolingLoad
parameters.description: Enter the proportion of the total load for heating or cooling that this system represents. The combined cooling load percentage for all cooling systems specified DOES NOT need to add up to 100%. If you set the cooling load to 70% and don't add any other cooling system, then the model assumes you're not conditioning 30% of the home's cooling load.

parameters.name: hvacPercentOfTotalCoolingLoadImproved
parameters.description: Enter the proportion of the total load for heating or cooling that this system represents. The combined cooling load percentage for all cooling systems specified DOES NOT need to add up to 100%. If you set the cooling load to 70% and don't add any other cooling system, then the model assumes you're not conditioning 30% of the home's cooling load.

parameters.name: hvacCoolingCapacity
parameters.description:
Enter the output capacity of the cooling equipment in (Btu/hr).  
  
Reminder: cooling systems are often rated in tons. 1 ton = 12,000 Btu/hr.  
  
**NOTE:** This has changed since v3 of Snugg Pro. v4 and later requires the number be in Btu/hr, not kBtu/hr. All v3 jobs that were migrated to v4 have been converted to the proper number by multiplying it by 1000.

parameters.name: hvacCoolingCapacityImproved
parameters.description:

**NOTE:** This has changed since v3 of Snugg Pro. v4 and later requires the number be in Btu/hr, not kBtu/hr. All v3 jobs that were migrated to v4 have been converted to the proper number by multiplying it by 1000.

parameters.name: hvacCoolingSystemEfficiency
parameters.description:
The Seasonal Energy Efficiency Ratio (SEER) is the average annual cooling efficiency of an air-conditioning or heat pump system as a weighted average of EERs over a range of rate outside air conditions following a standard test method. It includes energy of auxiliary systems such as the indoor and outdoor fans. Units: Btu/Wh.  
  
The Energy Efficiency Ratio (EER) is the measurement of the cooling capacity for a unit (in Btu/hour) divided by electrical energy it uses (in watts) at a specific temperature of 95ºF. Units: Btu/Wh. EER is only used for Room AC units.  
  
**Note:**  
Edit Base fields in the Details section only if you're confident of the actual value that is entered. The modeling engine utilizes utility data and input form data to determine the "effective" values on the Base side.  
  
Edit Improved fields in the Details section with the details of what you recommend be installed in the house. These values are only used if the item is Recommended. Default values are supplied by the modeling engine, but can be overridden.

parameters.name: hvacCoolingSystemEfficiencyImproved
parameters.description:
The Seasonal Energy Efficiency Ratio (SEER) is the average annual cooling efficiency of an air-conditioning or heat pump system as a weighted average of EERs over a range of rate outside air conditions following a standard test method. It includes energy of auxiliary systems such as the indoor and outdoor fans. Units: Btu/Wh.  
  
The Energy Efficiency Ratio (EER) is the measurement of the cooling capacity for a unit (in Btu/hour) divided by electrical energy it uses (in watts) at a specific temperature of 95ºF. Units: Btu/Wh. EER is only used for Room AC units.  
  
**Note:**  
Edit Base fields in the Details section only if you're confident of the actual value that is entered. The modeling engine utilizes utility data and input form data to determine the "effective" values on the Base side.  
  
Edit Improved fields in the Details section with the details of what you recommend be installed in the house. These values are only used if the item is Recommended. Default values are supplied by the modeling engine, but can be overridden.

parameters.name: hvacHeatingSystemEfficiency
parameters.description:
Annual Fuel Utilization Efficiency (AFUE) is a percentage representing the ratio of heat energy units provided to the total energy value of fuel consumed in identical units. It is most commonly used for combustion based heating as well as electric resistance heating.  
  
Heating Seasonal Performance Factor (HSPF) is the ratio of Btus of heat energy provided to the watt-hours of electricity consumed. It is most commonly used for air and ground source heat pumps.  
  
**Note:**  
Edit Base fields in the Details section only if you're confident of the actual value that is entered. The modeling engine utilizes utility data and input form data to determine the "effective" values on the Base side.  
  
Edit Improved fields in the Details section with the details of what you recommend be installed in the house. These values are only used if the item is Recommended. Default values are supplied by the modeling engine, but can be overridden.

parameters.name: hvacHeatingSystemEfficiencyImproved

Heating Seasonal Performance Factor (HSPF) is the ratio of Btus of heat energy provided to the watt-hours of electricity consumed. It is most commonly used for air and ground source heat pumps.  
  
**Note:**  
Edit Base fields in the Details section only if you're confident of the actual value that is entered. The modeling engine utilizes utility data and input form data to determine the "effective" values on the Base side.  
  
Edit Improved fields in the Details section with the details of what you recommend be installed in the house. These values are only used if the item is Recommended. Default values are supplied by the modeling engine, but can be overridden.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description:
**Recommend** a measure to feature it in the report's upgrade details section. This measure will factor into the job's total cost, savings and SIR.

**Note** a measure to list it in the report as 'Additional Notes'. Noted measures do not count towards the job's total cost, savings and SIR.

**Decline** a measure to hide it altogether report. Declined measures do not count towards the job's total cost, savings and SIR.

parameters.name: vaultModeledVaultArea
parameters.description:
This field will be automatically calculated based on the data you entered in the input form for Conditioned Area and Number of Stories. This is the actual surface area of the vaulted ceiling. It includes a default roof pitch of 5/12. When you override this number, be sure to include the complete sqft of the vault that's covering conditioned space.  
  
This field designates the interior surface area of the vaulted ceiling. On the improved side, this is the new total sqft of the vaulted ceiling. Increase this number if you're switching the attic from unconditioned to a conditioned space.  
  
For instance, if the original house had 1000 sqft of attic space and you converted it to a fully conditioned attic by spray foaming the roof deck and rafters, then set the base side to 1000 sqft and the improved side to 0. Then create a vaulted ceiling that is 0 on the base side and 1118 sqft on the improved side (adding 118 sqft for a 6/12 roof pitch).

parameters.name: vaultModeledVaultAreaImproved
parameters.description:
This field will be automatically calculated based on the data you entered in the input form for Conditioned Area and Number of Stories. This is the actual surface area of the vaulted ceiling. It includes a default roof pitch of 5/12. When you override this number, be sure to include the complete sqft of the vault that's covering conditioned space.  
  
This field designates the interior surface area of the vaulted ceiling. On the improved side, this is the new total sqft of the vaulted ceiling. Increase this number if you're switching the attic from unconditioned to a conditioned space.  
  
For instance, if the original house had 1000 sqft of attic space and you converted it to a fully conditioned attic by spray foaming the roof deck and rafters, then set the base side to 1000 sqft and the improved side to 0. Then create a vaulted ceiling that is 0 on the base side and 1118 sqft on the improved side (adding 118 sqft for a 6/12 roof pitch).

parameters.name: vaultCavityInsulation
parameters.description:
Enter the total R-value of insulation installed (BASE) or to be installed (IMPROVED) in this Vaulted Ceiling.  
  
**Note:**  
Edit Base fields in the Details section only if you're confident of the actual value that is entered. The modeling engine utilizes utility data and input form data to determine the "effective" values on the Base side.  
  
Edit Improved fields in the Details section with the details of what you recommend be installed in the house. These values are only used if the item is Recommended. Default values are supplied by the modeling engine, but can be overridden.

parameters.name: vaultContinuousInsulation
parameters.description:
Enter the total R-value of continuous insulation installed or to be installed in this Vaulted Ceiling. Continuous insulation is any insulation like spray foam or rigid foam that is continuous and consistent in R-value across studs, joists, or any framing member.  
  
**Note:**  
Edit Base fields in the Details section only if you're confident of the actual value that is entered. The modeling engine utilizes utility data and input form data to determine the "effective" values on the Base side.  
  
Edit Improved fields in the Details section with the details of what you recommend be installed in the house. These values are only used if the item is Recommended. Default values are supplied by the modeling engine, but can be overridden.

parameters.description:

Edit Base fields in the Details section only if you're confident of the actual value that is entered. The modeling engine utilizes utility data and input form data to determine the "effective" values on the Base side.  
  
Edit Improved fields in the Details section with the details of what you recommend be installed in the house. These values are only used if the item is Recommended. Default values are supplied by the modeling engine, but can be overridden.

parameters.name: vaultAssemblyRValue
parameters.description: This is the total assembly R-value for the entire vaulted ceiling system. We take the data from the Cavity and Continuous R-value fields and add factors for wood framing, drywall, air film, sheathing, roofing material etc. to calculate the overall assembly R-value. This field is not editable since it is calculated from the other fields.

parameters.name: vaultAssemblyRValueImproved
parameters.description: This is the total assembly R-value for the entire vaulted ceiling system. We take the data from the Cavity and Continuous R-value fields and add factors for wood framing, drywall, air film, sheathing, roofing material etc. to calculate the overall assembly R-value. This field is not editable since it is calculated from the other fields.

parameters.name: vault
parameters.description:
Is there insulation in the vaulted ceiling cavities of this home? If so, how good is it? If unsure, please leave blank.  
  
**Well**: All cavities contain insulation at full or near full depth with gaps and voids not exceeding 5%.  
  
**Poor**: Majority of cavities contain insulation, but one or more of the following is suspected: low density, low depth, poor installation.  
  
**Yes**: You can confirm that insulation exists, but are unsure of the installation quality.  
  
**No** - No insulation value is applied to cavities.

parameters.name: vaultPercent
parameters.description: Enter the % of the total footprint that is attributed to each attic or vault area.

parameters.name: vaultRoofAbsorptance
parameters.description: Enter the Solar Absorptance of the roofing material above this vaulted ceiling. Solar Absorptance is equal to 1 - Solar Reflectance. For example, if the ENERGY STAR website gives a Initial Solar Reflectance of 0.27, enter 0.73. Default numbers will be supplied automatically, so don't change these settings unless you plan to specify a cool roof as an improvement.

parameters.name: vaultRoofAbsorptanceImproved
parameters.description: Enter the Solar Absorptance of the roofing material above this vaulted ceiling. Solar Absorptance is equal to 1 - Solar Reflectance. For example, if the ENERGY STAR website gives a Initial Solar Reflectance of 0.27, enter 0.73. Default numbers will be supplied automatically, so don't change these settings unless you plan to specify a cool roof as an improvement.

parameters.name: vaultRoofEmissivity
parameters.description: Enter the Thermal Emittance or Emissivity of the roofing material above this vaulted ceiling. Default numbers will be supplied automatically, so don't change these settings unless you plan to specify a cool roof as an improvement.

parameters.name: vaultRoofEmissivityImproved
parameters.description: Enter the Thermal Emittance or Emissivity of the roofing material above this vaulted ceiling. Default numbers will be supplied automatically, so don't change these settings unless you plan to specify a cool roof as an improvement.

parameters.name: vaultCoolRoof
parameters.description: Choose yes if you would like to specify characteristics of a cool roof (such as Whole Home Energy Solutions Qualified roof products) for either the base or improved home. Default numbers will be supplied automatically. Most contractors will not use this option unless they are actually planning to replace a roof as part of an improvement package.

parameters.name: vaultCoolRoofImproved

Default numbers will be supplied automatically. Most contractors will not use this option unless they are actually planning to replace a roof as part of an improvement package.

parameters.name: wallAssemblyRValue

This field is not editable since it is calculated from the other fields.

parameters.name: wallAssemblyRValueImproved
parameters.description: This is the total assembly R-value for the entire basement wall system. We take the data from the Cavity and Continuous R-value fields and add factors for wood framing, drywall, air film, etc. to calculate the overall assembly R-value. This field is not editable since it is calculated from the other fields.

parameters.name: wallModeledWallArea
parameters.description:
This field will be automatically calculated based on the data you entered in the input form for House Length, House Width, and Average Wall Height.  
  
This field designates the total gross exterior wall area, including all window and door openings. It does not include walls shared in common with other conditioned dwelling units. An additional 1' of wall height is automatically included for every story beyond the first to account for rim joists. First floor (foundation) rim joists are included separately in the basement or crawl space wall areas.  
  
The BASE area designates the sqft of this wall system on the house. The IMPROVED area is the actual sqft of surface area in the new home. Changing the IMPROVED area will reduce or increase the sqft of wall area in the home. If you want to improve only a portion of the walls, then you must build a second wall system where you improve one of the systems, but not the other.

parameters.name: wallModeledWallAreaImproved
parameters.description:
This field will be automatically calculated based on the data you entered in the input form for House Length, House Width, and Average Wall Height.  
  
This field designates the total gross exterior wall area, including all window and door openings. It does not include walls shared in common with other conditioned dwelling units. An additional 1' of wall height is automatically included for every story beyond the first to account for rim joists. First floor (foundation) rim joists are included separately in the basement or crawl space wall areas.  
  
The BASE area designates the sqft of this wall system on the house. The IMPROVED area is the actual sqft of surface area in the new home. Changing the IMPROVED area will reduce or increase the sqft of wall area in the home. If you want to improve only a portion of the walls, then you must build a second wall system where you improve one of the systems, but not the other.

The IMPROVED values represent the area of windows in this orientation that are actually going to be improved. The difference in sqft between BASE and IMPROVED will be assumed as unchanged. **Note:**  
Edit Base fields in the Details section only if you're confident of the actual value that is entered. The modeling engine utilizes utility data and input form data to determine the "effective" values on the Base side.  
  
Edit Improved fields in the Details section with the details of what you recommend be installed in the house. These values are only used if the item is Recommended. Default values are supplied by the modeling engine, but can be overridden.

parameters.name: windowAreaWestImproved
parameters.description:

parameters.name: windowSolarHeatGainCoefficient
parameters.description:
Enter the average gross window (including frame effects) Solar Heat Gain Coefficient (SHGC) of the windows installed (BASE) or to be installed (IMPROVED). IMPORTANT! Do not adjust this number to account for the affects of solar screens or shades. Use the Exterior Treatment fields below for this and only change the SHGC if the window unit itself is being replaced.  
  
**Note:**  
Edit Base fields in the Details section only if you're confident of the actual value that is entered. The modeling engine utilizes utility data and input form data to determine the "effective" values on the Base side.  
  
Edit Improved fields in the Details section with the details of what you recommend be installed in the house. These values are only used if the item is Recommended. Default values are supplied by the modeling engine, but can be overridden.
