parameters.name: atticInsulationDepth
parameters.description: Enter the depth of all installed attic or ceiling insulation. If the insulation is not evenly distributed, estimate an average depth for the area. If the insulation is evenly distributed but has different depths installed in different area, use the depth of insulation that covers the largest area. If there is no attic and instead vaulted ceilings, enter an estimate of the thickness of insulation in the vaulted cavity.

parameters.name: atticInsulationType
parameters.description: Select the type of insulation that is installed in the attic or ceiling. If more than one type of insulation is installed, select the type that covers that largest area.

parameters.name: atticInsulationTypeImproved
parameters.description: Select the type of insulation that is installed in the attic or ceiling. If more than one type of insulation is installed, select the type that covers that largest area.

parameters.name: atticModeledAtticArea
parameters.description:
This field will be automatically calculated based on the data you entered in the input form for Conditioned Area and Number of Stories.  
  
This field designates the actual interior surface area of the ceiling. On the improved side, this is the new total sqft of the attic. Reduce this number if you're switching the attic from unconditioned to a conditioned space. For instance, if the original house had 1000 sqft of attic space and you converted it to a fully conditioned attic by spray foaming the roof deck and rafters, then set the base side to 1000 sqft and the improved side to 0. Then create a vaulted ceiling that is 0 on the base side and 1118 sqft on the improved side (adding 118 sqft for a 6/12 roof pitch).

parameters.name: atticModeledAtticAreaImproved
parameters.description:
This field will be automatically calculated based on the data you entered in the input form for Conditioned Area and Number of Stories.  
  
This field designates the actual interior surface area of the ceiling. On the improved side, this is the new total sqft of the attic. Reduce this number if you're switching the attic from unconditioned to a conditioned space. For instance, if the original house had 1000 sqft of attic space and you converted it to a fully conditioned attic by spray foaming the roof deck and rafters, then set the base side to 1000 sqft and the improved side to 0. Then create a vaulted ceiling that is 0 on the base side and 1118 sqft on the improved side (adding 118 sqft for a 6/12 roof pitch).

parameters.name: atticInsulation
parameters.description:
Enter the total R-value of insulation installed (BASE) or to be installed (IMPROVED) in this Attic. Framing factors have already been taken into account, so specify the R-value of the insulation within the cavity.  
  
**Note:**  
Edit Base fields in the Details section only if you're confident of the actual value that is entered. The modeling engine utilizes utility data and input form data to determine the "effective" values on the Base side.  
  
Edit Improved fields in the Details section with the details of what you recommend be installed in the house. These values are only used if the item is Recommended. Default values are supplied by the modeling engine, but can be overridden.

parameters.name: atticInsulationImproved
parameters.description:
Enter the total R-value of insulation installed (BASE) or to be installed (IMPROVED) in this Attic. Framing factors have already been taken into account, so specify the R-value of the insulation within the cavity.  
  
**Note:**  
Edit Base fields in the Details section only if you're confident of the actual value that is entered. The modeling engine utilizes utility data and input form data to determine the "effective" values on the Base side.  
  
Edit Improved fields in the Details section with the details of what you recommend be installed in the house. These values are only used if the item is Recommended. Default values are supplied by the modeling engine, but can be overridden.

parameters.name: atticAssemblyRValue
parameters.description: This is the total assembly R-value for the entire attic floor system. We take the data from the Attic Insulation field and add factors for wood framing, drywall, air film, etc. to calculate the overall assembly R-value. This field is not editable since it is calculated from the other fields.

parameters.name: atticAssemblyRValueImproved
parameters.description: This is the total assembly R-value for the entire attic floor system. We take the data from the Attic Insulation field and add factors for wood framing, drywall, air film, etc. to calculate the overall assembly R-value. This field is not editable since it is calculated from the other fields.

parameters.name: atticKneeWallArea
parameters.description: Enter the total surface area of knee wall adjoining this attic space.

parameters.name: atticKneeWallAreaImproved
parameters.description: Enter the total surface area of knee wall adjoining this attic space.

parameters.name: atticKneeWallInsulation
parameters.description: Enter the total R-value of insulation installed (BASE) or to be installed (IMPROVED) in this Knee Wall cavity.

parameters.name: atticKneeWallInsulationImproved
parameters.description: Enter the total R-value of insulation installed (BASE) or to be installed (IMPROVED) in this Knee Wall cavity.

parameters.name: atticKneeWallContinuousInsulation
parameters.description: Enter the total R-value of insulation installed (BASE) or to be installed (IMPROVED) in this Knee Wall cavity.

parameters.name: atticKneeWallContinuousInsulationImproved
parameters.description: Enter the total R-value of insulation installed (BASE) or to be installed (IMPROVED) in this Knee Wall cavity.

parameters.name: atticKneeWallAssemblyRValue
parameters.description: This is the total assembly R-value for the entire knee wall system. We take the data from the Cavity and Continuous R-value fields and add factors for wood framing, drywall, air film, etc. to calculate the overall assembly R-value. This field is not editable since it is calculated from the other fields.

parameters.name: atticKneeWallAssemblyRValueImproved
parameters.description: This is the total assembly R-value for the entire knee wall system. We take the data from the Cavity and Continuous R-value fields and add factors for wood framing, drywall, air film, etc. to calculate the overall assembly R-value. This field is not editable since it is calculated from the other fields.

parameters.description: Choose Yes if there is a knee wall in this attic space.

parameters.name: atticHasKneeWallImproved
parameters.description: Choose Yes if there is a knee wall in this attic space.

parameters.name: atticRadiantBarrier
parameters.description: Please designate if the base or improved attic has a radiant barrier. Costs will not be adjusted for this nor will the R-Value of the attic insulation. The energy savings will be calculated in addition to the R-Value of the attic.

parameters.name: atticRadiantBarrierImproved
parameters.description: Please designate if the base or improved attic has a radiant barrier. Costs will not be adjusted for this nor will the R-Value of the attic insulation. The energy savings will be calculated in addition to the R-Value of the attic.

parameters.name: atticPercent
parameters.description: Enter the % of the total footprint that is attributed to each attic or vault area.

parameters.name: atticRoofAbsorptance
parameters.description: Enter the Solar Absorptance of the roofing material above this attic space. Solar Absorptance is equal to 1 - Solar Reflectance. For example, if the ENERGY STAR website gives a Initial Solar Reflectance of 0.27, enter 0.73. Default numbers will be supplied automatically, so don't change these settings unless you plan to specify a cool roof as an improvement.

parameters.name: atticRoofAbsorptanceImproved
parameters.description: Enter the Solar Absorptance of the roofing material above this attic space. Solar Absorptance is equal to 1 - Solar Reflectance. For example, if the ENERGY STAR website gives a Initial Solar Reflectance of 0.27, enter 0.73. Default numbers will be supplied automatically, so don't change these settings unless you plan to specify a cool roof as an improvement.

parameters.name: atticRoofEmissivity
parameters.description: Enter the Thermal Emittance or Emissivity of the roofing material above this attic space. Default numbers will be supplied automatically, so don't change these settings unless you plan to specify a cool roof as an improvement.

parameters.name: atticRoofEmissivityImproved
parameters.description: Enter the Thermal Emittance or Emissivity of the roofing material above this attic space. Default numbers will be supplied automatically, so don't change these settings unless you plan to specify a cool roof as an improvement.

parameters.name: atticCoolRoof
parameters.description: Choose yes if you would like to specify characteristics of a cool roof (such as Whole Home Energy Solutions Qualified roof products) for either the base or improved home. Default numbers will be supplied automatically. Most contractors will not use this option unless they are actually planning to replace a roof as part of an improvement package.

parameters.name: atticCoolRoofImproved
parameters.description: Choose yes if you would like to specify characteristics of a cool roof (such as Whole Home Energy Solutions Qualified roof products) for either the base or improved home. Default numbers will be supplied automatically. Most contractors will not use this option unless they are actually planning to replace a roof as part of an improvement package.

parameters.name: hesDhwEnergyFactor
parameters.description: The efficiency of a storage water heater is indicated by its Energy Factor (EF). If you know or have a good idea what the EF is, (check the EnergyGuide label on the water heater) enter it here, otherwise enter an installed year. This number should be entered as a decimal (e.g. 0.55). The allowed range is 0.45 to 4.0. Combustion water heaters are typically between 0.5 and 0.65. Electric are typically between 0.8 and 0.95. Heat pump water heaters may be as high as 4.

parameters.name: hvacSystemName
parameters.description: Give this system a name that you can use to easily differentiate it from the others like: Old Heat Pump or Basement Furnace.

parameters.name: hvacUpgradeAction
enum: Keep an existing system as is
parameters.description:
Choose an action that you plan to take for this HVAC system.**Replace with a newer model:**  
Replace the existing equipment with a new version of the same thing. Eg. replace a 78 AFUE furnace with a new 98 AFUE furnace  
**Keep an existing system as is:**  
Do nothing to a system or just change the percent of total load that that old system covers in the house.  
**Remove a system permanently:**  
Rip out an entire system and replace it with something completely new, like going from a direct heater and room AC to a central heat pump. In this instance you will have a system that is described on the base side, and the improved side will be blank.  
**Install a new non-existing system:**  
A brand new system that replaces the system that was removed when you chose: Remove a system permanently.

parameters.name: hvacHeatingIsCondensing

Setting this to true will set the default a calibrated number between 90 and 96 AFUE automatically.

parameters.name: hvacCoolingSystemEfficiency
parameters.description:
The Seasonal Energy Efficiency Ratio (SEER) is the average annual cooling efficiency of an air-conditioning or heat pump system as a weighted average of EERs over a range of rate outside air conditions following a standard test method. It includes energy of auxiliary systems such as the indoor and outdoor fans. Units: Btu/Wh.  
  
The Energy Efficiency Ratio (EER) is the measurement of the cooling capacity for a unit (in Btu/hour) divided by electrical energy it uses (in watts) at a specific temperature of 95ºF. Units: Btu/Wh. EER is only used for Room AC units.  
  
**Note:**  
Edit Base fields in the Details section only if you're confident of the actual value that is entered. The modeling engine utilizes utility data and input form data to determine the "effective" values on the Base side.  
  
Edit Improved fields in the Details section with the details of what you recommend be installed in the house. These values are only used if the item is Recommended. Default values are supplied by the modeling engine, but can be overridden.

parameters.name: hvacCoolingSystemEfficiencyImproved
parameters.description:

It includes energy of auxiliary systems such as the indoor and outdoor fans. Units: Btu/Wh.  
  
The Energy Efficiency Ratio (EER) is the measurement of the cooling capacity for a unit (in Btu/hour) divided by electrical energy it uses (in watts) at a specific temperature of 95ºF. Units: Btu/Wh. EER is only used for Room AC units.  
  
**Note:**

parameters.name: hvacHeatingSystemEfficiency
parameters.description:
Annual Fuel Utilization Efficiency (AFUE) is a percentage representing the ratio of heat energy units provided to the total energy value of fuel consumed in identical units. It is most commonly used for combustion based heating as well as electric resistance heating.  
  
Heating Seasonal Performance Factor (HSPF) is the ratio of Btus of heat energy provided to the watt-hours of electricity consumed. It is most commonly used for air and ground source heat pumps.  
  
**Note:**  
Edit Base fields in the Details section only if you're confident of the actual value that is entered. The modeling engine utilizes utility data and input form data to determine the "effective" values on the Base side.  
  
Edit Improved fields in the Details section with the details of what you recommend be installed in the house. These values are only used if the item is Recommended. Default values are supplied by the modeling engine, but can be overridden.

**Note:**

parameters.description:
This field will be automatically calculated based on the data you entered in the input form for Conditioned Area and Number of Stories. This is the actual surface area of the vaulted ceiling. It includes a default roof pitch of 5/12. When you override this number, be sure to include the complete sqft of the vault that's covering conditioned space.  
  
This field designates the interior surface area of the vaulted ceiling. On the improved side, this is the new total sqft of the vaulted ceiling. Increase this number if you're switching the attic from unconditioned to a conditioned space.  
  
For instance, if the original house had 1000 sqft of attic space and you converted it to a fully conditioned attic by spray foaming the roof deck and rafters, then set the base side to 1000 sqft and the improved side to 0. Then create a vaulted ceiling that is 0 on the base side and 1118 sqft on the improved side (adding 118 sqft for a 6/12 roof pitch).

parameters.name: vaultModeledVaultAreaImproved
parameters.description:
This field will be automatically calculated based on the data you entered in the input form for Conditioned Area and Number of Stories. This is the actual surface area of the vaulted ceiling. It includes a default roof pitch of 5/12. When you override this number, be sure to include the complete sqft of the vault that's covering conditioned space.  
  
This field designates the interior surface area of the vaulted ceiling. On the improved side, this is the new total sqft of the vaulted ceiling. Increase this number if you're switching the attic from unconditioned to a conditioned space.  
  
For instance, if the original house had 1000 sqft of attic space and you converted it to a fully conditioned attic by spray foaming the roof deck and rafters, then set the base side to 1000 sqft and the improved side to 0. Then create a vaulted ceiling that is 0 on the base side and 1118 sqft on the improved side (adding 118 sqft for a 6/12 roof pitch).

parameters.name: vaultCavityInsulation
parameters.description:
Enter the total R-value of insulation installed (BASE) or to be installed (IMPROVED) in this Vaulted Ceiling.  
  
**Note:**  
Edit Base fields in the Details section only if you're confident of the actual value that is entered. The modeling engine utilizes utility data and input form data to determine the "effective" values on the Base side.  
  
Edit Improved fields in the Details section with the details of what you recommend be installed in the house. These values are only used if the item is Recommended. Default values are supplied by the modeling engine, but can be overridden.

parameters.name: vaultContinuousInsulation

**Note:**  
Edit Base fields in the Details section only if you're confident of the actual value that is entered. The modeling engine utilizes utility data and input form data to determine the "effective" values on the Base side.  
  
Edit Improved fields in the Details section with the details of what you recommend be installed in the house. These values are only used if the item is Recommended. Default values are supplied by the modeling engine, but can be overridden.

parameters.name: vaultAssemblyRValue
parameters.description: This is the total assembly R-value for the entire vaulted ceiling system. We take the data from the Cavity and Continuous R-value fields and add factors for wood framing, drywall, air film, sheathing, roofing material etc. to calculate the overall assembly R-value. This field is not editable since it is calculated from the other fields.

parameters.name: vaultAssemblyRValueImproved
parameters.description: This is the total assembly R-value for the entire vaulted ceiling system. We take the data from the Cavity and Continuous R-value fields and add factors for wood framing, drywall, air film, sheathing, roofing material etc. to calculate the overall assembly R-value. This field is not editable since it is calculated from the other fields.

parameters.name: vault
parameters.description:
Is there insulation in the vaulted ceiling cavities of this home? If so, how good is it? If unsure, please leave blank.  
  
**Well**: All cavities contain insulation at full or near full depth with gaps and voids not exceeding 5%.  
  
**Poor**: Majority of cavities contain insulation, but one or more of the following is suspected: low density, low depth, poor installation.  
  
**Yes**: You can confirm that insulation exists, but are unsure of the installation quality.  
  
**No** - No insulation value is applied to cavities.

parameters.name: vaultPercent
parameters.description: Enter the % of the total footprint that is attributed to each attic or vault area.

parameters.name: vaultRoofAbsorptance
parameters.description: Enter the Solar Absorptance of the roofing material above this vaulted ceiling. Solar Absorptance is equal to 1 - Solar Reflectance. For example, if the ENERGY STAR website gives a Initial Solar Reflectance of 0.27, enter 0.73. Default numbers will be supplied automatically, so don't change these settings unless you plan to specify a cool roof as an improvement.

parameters.name: vaultRoofAbsorptanceImproved
parameters.description: Enter the Solar Absorptance of the roofing material above this vaulted ceiling. Solar Absorptance is equal to 1 - Solar Reflectance. For example, if the ENERGY STAR website gives a Initial Solar Reflectance of 0.27, enter 0.73. Default numbers will be supplied automatically, so don't change these settings unless you plan to specify a cool roof as an improvement.

parameters.name: vaultRoofEmissivity
parameters.description: Enter the Thermal Emittance or Emissivity of the roofing material above this vaulted ceiling. Default numbers will be supplied automatically, so don't change these settings unless you plan to specify a cool roof as an improvement.

parameters.name: vaultRoofEmissivityImproved
parameters.description: Enter the Thermal Emittance or Emissivity of the roofing material above this vaulted ceiling. Default numbers will be supplied automatically, so don't change these settings unless you plan to specify a cool roof as an improvement.

parameters.name: vaultCoolRoof
parameters.description: Choose yes if you would like to specify characteristics of a cool roof (such as Whole Home Energy Solutions Qualified roof products) for either the base or improved home. Default numbers will be supplied automatically. Most contractors will not use this option unless they are actually planning to replace a roof as part of an improvement package.

parameters.name: vaultCoolRoofImproved
parameters.description: Choose yes if you would like to specify characteristics of a cool roof (such as Whole Home Energy Solutions Qualified roof products) for either the base or improved home. Default numbers will be supplied automatically. Most contractors will not use this option unless they are actually planning to replace a roof as part of an improvement package.

parameters.name: wallAssemblyRValue

This field is not editable since it is calculated from the other fields.

parameters.name: wallAssemblyRValueImproved
parameters.description: This is the total assembly R-value for the entire basement wall system. We take the data from the Cavity and Continuous R-value fields and add factors for wood framing, drywall, air film, etc. to calculate the overall assembly R-value. This field is not editable since it is calculated from the other fields.

parameters.name: wallModeledWallArea
parameters.description:
This field will be automatically calculated based on the data you entered in the input form for House Length, House Width, and Average Wall Height.  
  
This field designates the total gross exterior wall area, including all window and door openings. It does not include walls shared in common with other conditioned dwelling units. An additional 1' of wall height is automatically included for every story beyond the first to account for rim joists. First floor (foundation) rim joists are included separately in the basement or crawl space wall areas.  
  
The BASE area designates the sqft of this wall system on the house. The IMPROVED area is the actual sqft of surface area in the new home. Changing the IMPROVED area will reduce or increase the sqft of wall area in the home. If you want to improve only a portion of the walls, then you must build a second wall system where you improve one of the systems, but not the other.

parameters.name: wallModeledWallAreaImproved
parameters.description:
This field will be automatically calculated based on the data you entered in the input form for House Length, House Width, and Average Wall Height.  
  
This field designates the total gross exterior wall area, including all window and door openings. It does not include walls shared in common with other conditioned dwelling units. An additional 1' of wall height is automatically included for every story beyond the first to account for rim joists. First floor (foundation) rim joists are included separately in the basement or crawl space wall areas.  
  
The BASE area designates the sqft of this wall system on the house. The IMPROVED area is the actual sqft of surface area in the new home. Changing the IMPROVED area will reduce or increase the sqft of wall area in the home. If you want to improve only a portion of the walls, then you must build a second wall system where you improve one of the systems, but not the other.

parameters.name: healthAmbientCarbonMonoxide
parameters.description: Choose the appropriate description for this test. If you performed an actual test and it passed, then choose "Passed", if the test failed, then choose "Failed". If you did not perform this test, but suspect there might be issues or have notes to provide the homeowner or contractor, then choose "Warning". If you did not perform this test and do not want the test information to show up on the homeowner report, then choose "Not Tested".

If you did not perform this test and do not want the test information to show up on the homeowner report, then choose "Not Tested".

parameters.name: billEntryType
parameters.description:
Choose a method of utility bill entry. Always use detailed bills if you have 13 months of meter read dates with associated fuel and electricity usage. Detailed bills will provide more accurate energy savings estimates. If you can get monthly bills that cover the primary heating and cooling season, then choose detailed bills.  
  
Simple Bills can be used if the home has not been occupied by the current occupants for more than 13 months or you don't have access to detailed bills for the current occupants. You can typically get the high and low bills for gas and electric for any address by calling the utility's customer service number and requesting it (even without permission of the tenants at the time).

parameters.name: electricUtilityProviderName
parameters.description: Enter the name of the electric utility provider. This is an optional field for most users, but could be required for certain efficiency programs.

parameters.name: electricAccountNumber
parameters.description: Enter the utility account number for the meter that these bills correspond to. This is an optional field for most users, but could be required for certain efficiency programs.

parameters.name: highestMonthlySummerElectricBill
parameters.description:
Enter the amount of the highest monthly summertime electricity bill for the home during the past 12 months. Summertime is typically those months when many buildings would use air conditioning to cool the building.  
  
If the home is on a budget plan where the monthly electricity bills are all the same, the highest bill can be estimated by looking for the summer month with the highest electricity usage and then multiplying that amount by the electricity rate being charged.  
  
Notes:  
Applicable to bills of `Simple` entry type

parameters.name: lowestMonthlyElectricBill
parameters.description:
Enter the amount of the lowest of all the monthly electricity bills for the home during the past 12 months. If the home is on a budget plan where the monthly electricity bills are all the same, the lowest bill can be estimated by looking for the month with the lowest electricity usage and then multiplying that amount by the electricity rate being charged.  
  
Notes:  
Applicable to bills of `Simple` entry type

parameters.name: electricBillUnits
parameters.description:
Enter the units used for the electric bills. This is a required field if you're going to use Detailed Bills. For best results, utilize the kWh option instead of dollars for more accurate representations of the home.  
  
Notes:  
Applicable to bills of `Detailed` entry type

parameters.name: startElectricDate1
parameters.description:
**Start with the oldest bill first.**  
  
It's very important to enter meter read dates as opposed to billing dates.  

*   If you have 13 months of utility bills, enter the meter read date of the oldest of the 13 bills.
*   If you only have 12 months of utility bills, look at the oldest bill. Search for a previous meter read date. Enter that here.  
    If you can't find a previous meter read date, then search "number of days in billing period". Enter a date that's this number of days prior to the meter read date you're looking at.

_See the knowledge base for more information on entering utility bills._  
  
Notes:  
Applicable to bills of `Detailed` entry type

parameters.name: endElectricDate1
parameters.description:
*   If you only have 12 months of utility bills, enter the meter read date for **the oldest bill** you have.
*   If you have 13 months of bills, enter the meter read date and usage for the **2nd oldest bill**.
*   Then enter the energy usage from the corresponding date to the left. Be sure that this usage is for the time period between the End Bill Date to the left and the previous date in this list.

  
  
Notes:  
Applicable to bills of `Detailed` entry type

parameters.name: endElectricBill1
parameters.description:
Notes:  
Applicable to bills of `Detailed` entry type

parameters.name: endElectricDate2
parameters.description:
*   Fill out as many of these meter read dates as you have available.
*   This detailed input section can be used with any amount of available data.
*   Enter zero for missing or abnormal bills. The utility analysis will treat an entry of zero as missing and will do its best to compensate for missing bills.
*   To include a bill with actual zero usage, enter 0.01.
*   Be sure that you're entering the usage for the corresponding measurement that you designated in the Bill Units above (ie. dollars or kWh).

  
  
Notes:  
Applicable to bills of `Detailed` entry type

*   Then enter the energy usage from the corresponding date to the left. Be sure that this usage is for the time period between the End Bill Date to the left and the previous date in this list.

  
  
Notes:  
Applicable to bills of `Detailed` entry type

parameters.name: primaryHeatingFuelType
parameters.description:
This is the primary fuel source. It is used to set prices and energy content.  
  
Notes:  
Applicable to bills of `Detailed` entry type

parameters.name: fuelUtilityProviderName
parameters.description: Enter the name of the fuel utility provider. This is an optional field for most users, but could be required for certain efficiency programs.

parameters.name: fuelAccountNumber
parameters.description: Enter the utility account number for the meter that the fuel bills correspond to. This is an optional field for most users, but could be required for certain efficiency programs.

parameters.name: additionalFuelUtilityProviderName
parameters.description: Enter the name of an additional fuel utility provider if applicable. This is an optional field for most users, but could be required for certain efficiency programs.

parameters.name: additionalFuelAccountNumber
parameters.description: Enter the utility additional account number for the meter that the fuel bills correspond to. This is an optional field for most users, but could be required for certain efficiency programs.

parameters.name: highestMonthlyWinterElectricBill
parameters.description:
Enter the amount of the highest monthly winter electric bill for the home during the past 12 months.  
  
If the home is on a budget plan where the monthly electricity bills are all the same, the highest bill can be estimated by looking for the winter month with the highest electricity usage and then multiplying that amount by the electricity rate being charged.  
  
Notes:  
Applicable to bills of `Simple` entry type

parameters.name: highestMonthlyWinterNaturalGasBill
parameters.description:
Enter the amount of the highest monthly winter gas bill for the home during the past 12 months.  
  
If the home is on a budget plan where the monthly gas bills are all the same, the highest bill can be estimated by looking for the winter month with the highest gas usage and then multiplying that amount by the gas rate being charged. If the home is heated by more than one type of fuel, use the bill from the type which provides the heat for the largest area of the home.  
  
Notes:  
Applicable to bills of `Simple` entry type

parameters.name: lowestMonthlyNaturalGasBill
parameters.description:
This is typically in the summer time when you're only using fuel for hot water and cooking.  
  
If the home uses more than one type, use the bill from the type which is used the most within the home.  
  
Notes:  
Applicable to bills of `Simple` entry type

parameters.name: simpleFuelUnits
parameters.description:
Notes:  
Applicable to bills of `Simple` entry type

parameters.name: totalSimpleFuelUsedInLast12Months
parameters.description:
Take a look at your delivery receipts and add up the total gallons delivered over the past 12 months. If you don't have information on the gallons, you can look at bank statements, etc. to total up how much you've spent on deliveries over the past 12 months.  
  
Notes:  
Applicable to bills of `Simple` entry type

parameters.name: fuelBillUnits
parameters.description:
The units used for the fuel bills. This is a required field if you're going to use Detailed Bills. For best results, utilize the kWh option instead of dollars for more accurate representations of the home.  
  
Notes:  
Applicable to bills of `Detailed` entry type

parameters.name: detailedFuelDataSource

parameters.name: utilityPricePropane

parameters.name: utilityPriceFuelOil

parameters.name: utilityPriceElectricity
parameters.description: Enter a your own price for Electricity to override the software's existing regional defaults from the EIA.

parameters.name: utilityPriceWood
parameters.description: Enter a your own price for wood. We do not have default costs for wood, therefore this must be filled out unless the wood supply is free.

parameters.name: utilityPricePellets
parameters.description: Enter a your own price for wood pellets. We do not have default costs for wood pellets, therefore this must be filled out if pellets are chosen in the heating system fuel options.

parameters.name: utilityPriceCoal
parameters.description: Enter your own price for coal. We do not have default costs for coal, therefore this must be filled out if coal is chosen in heating fuel options.

parameters.name: utilityPriceKerosene
parameters.description: Enter your own price for kerosene. We do not have default costs for kerosene, therefore this must be filled out if kerosene is chosen in heating fuel options.

parameters.name: fuelRatePlan
parameters.description: Enter the name of the utility fuel rate plan. For gas, the same rate plan is used for both Base and Improved; separate Improved Fuel Rate Plans are not supported.

stageId.description:
Current stage ID of the job.  
Field options:  
1 → Lead  
2 → Audit  
3 → Bid Proposed  
4 → Bid Approved  
5 → Retrofit In Progress  
6 → Retrofit Complete  
7 → QA  
8 → Uncategorized  
9 → Archived Won  
10 → Archived Lost

hpxmlUrl.description: URL to download the HPXML, including a signed token good for 12 hours

uuid.description: This is a universally unique ID for this item (UUID).

atticInsulationDepth.description: Enter the depth of all installed attic or ceiling insulation. If the insulation is not evenly distributed, estimate an average depth for the area. If the insulation is evenly distributed but has different depths installed in different area, use the depth of insulation that covers the largest area. If there is no attic and instead vaulted ceilings, enter an estimate of the thickness of insulation in the vaulted cavity.

atticInsulationType.description: Select the type of insulation that is installed in the attic or ceiling. If more than one type of insulation is installed, select the type that covers that largest area.

atticInsulationTypeImproved.description: Select the type of insulation that is installed in the attic or ceiling. If more than one type of insulation is installed, select the type that covers that largest area.

atticModeledAtticArea.description:
This field will be automatically calculated based on the data you entered in the input form for Conditioned Area and Number of Stories.  
  
This field designates the actual interior surface area of the ceiling. On the improved side, this is the new total sqft of the attic. Reduce this number if you're switching the attic from unconditioned to a conditioned space. For instance, if the original house had 1000 sqft of attic space and you converted it to a fully conditioned attic by spray foaming the roof deck and rafters, then set the base side to 1000 sqft and the improved side to 0. Then create a vaulted ceiling that is 0 on the base side and 1118 sqft on the improved side (adding 118 sqft for a 6/12 roof pitch).

atticModeledAtticAreaImproved.description:
This field will be automatically calculated based on the data you entered in the input form for Conditioned Area and Number of Stories.  
  
This field designates the actual interior surface area of the ceiling. On the improved side, this is the new total sqft of the attic. Reduce this number if you're switching the attic from unconditioned to a conditioned space. For instance, if the original house had 1000 sqft of attic space and you converted it to a fully conditioned attic by spray foaming the roof deck and rafters, then set the base side to 1000 sqft and the improved side to 0. Then create a vaulted ceiling that is 0 on the base side and 1118 sqft on the improved side (adding 118 sqft for a 6/12 roof pitch).

atticInsulation.description:
Enter the total R-value of insulation installed (BASE) or to be installed (IMPROVED) in this Attic. Framing factors have already been taken into account, so specify the R-value of the insulation within the cavity.  
  
**Note:**  
Edit Base fields in the Details section only if you're confident of the actual value that is entered. The modeling engine utilizes utility data and input form data to determine the "effective" values on the Base side.  
  
Edit Improved fields in the Details section with the details of what you recommend be installed in the house. These values are only used if the item is Recommended. Default values are supplied by the modeling engine, but can be overridden.

atticInsulationImproved.description:
Enter the total R-value of insulation installed (BASE) or to be installed (IMPROVED) in this Attic. Framing factors have already been taken into account, so specify the R-value of the insulation within the cavity.  
  
**Note:**  
Edit Base fields in the Details section only if you're confident of the actual value that is entered. The modeling engine utilizes utility data and input form data to determine the "effective" values on the Base side.  
  
Edit Improved fields in the Details section with the details of what you recommend be installed in the house. These values are only used if the item is Recommended. Default values are supplied by the modeling engine, but can be overridden.

atticAssemblyRValue.description: This is the total assembly R-value for the entire attic floor system. We take the data from the Attic Insulation field and add factors for wood framing, drywall, air film, etc. to calculate the overall assembly R-value. This field is not editable since it is calculated from the other fields.

atticAssemblyRValueImproved.description: This is the total assembly R-value for the entire attic floor system. We take the data from the Attic Insulation field and add factors for wood framing, drywall, air film, etc. to calculate the overall assembly R-value. This field is not editable since it is calculated from the other fields.

atticKneeWallArea.description: Enter the total surface area of knee wall adjoining this attic space.

atticKneeWallAreaImproved.description: Enter the total surface area of knee wall adjoining this attic space.

atticKneeWallInsulation.description: Enter the total R-value of insulation installed (BASE) or to be installed (IMPROVED) in this Knee Wall cavity.

atticKneeWallInsulationImproved.description: Enter the total R-value of insulation installed (BASE) or to be installed (IMPROVED) in this Knee Wall cavity.

atticKneeWallContinuousInsulation.description: Enter the total R-value of insulation installed (BASE) or to be installed (IMPROVED) in this Knee Wall cavity.

atticKneeWallContinuousInsulationImprove.description: Enter the total R-value of insulation installed (BASE) or to be installed (IMPROVED) in this Knee Wall cavity.

atticKneeWallAssemblyRValue.description: This is the total assembly R-value for the entire knee wall system. We take the data from the Cavity and Continuous R-value fields and add factors for wood framing, drywall, air film, etc. to calculate the overall assembly R-value. This field is not editable since it is calculated from the other fields.

atticKneeWallAssemblyRValueImproved.description: This is the total assembly R-value for the entire knee wall system. We take the data from the Cavity and Continuous R-value fields and add factors for wood framing, drywall, air film, etc. to calculate the overall assembly R-value. This field is not editable since it is calculated from the other fields.

atticHasKneeWall.description: Choose Yes if there is a knee wall in this attic space.

atticHasKneeWallImproved.description: Choose Yes if there is a knee wall in this attic space.

atticRadiantBarrier.description: Please designate if the base or improved attic has a radiant barrier. Costs will not be adjusted for this nor will the R-Value of the attic insulation. The energy savings will be calculated in addition to the R-Value of the attic.

atticRadiantBarrierImproved.description: Please designate if the base or improved attic has a radiant barrier. Costs will not be adjusted for this nor will the R-Value of the attic insulation. The energy savings will be calculated in addition to the R-Value of the attic.

atticPercent.description: Enter the % of the total footprint that is attributed to each attic or vault area.

atticRoofAbsorptance.description: Enter the Solar Absorptance of the roofing material above this attic space. Solar Absorptance is equal to 1 - Solar Reflectance. For example, if the ENERGY STAR website gives a Initial Solar Reflectance of 0.27, enter 0.73. Default numbers will be supplied automatically, so don't change these settings unless you plan to specify a cool roof as an improvement.

atticRoofAbsorptanceImproved.description: Enter the Solar Absorptance of the roofing material above this attic space. Solar Absorptance is equal to 1 - Solar Reflectance. For example, if the ENERGY STAR website gives a Initial Solar Reflectance of 0.27, enter 0.73. Default numbers will be supplied automatically, so don't change these settings unless you plan to specify a cool roof as an improvement.

atticRoofEmissivity.description: Enter the Thermal Emittance or Emissivity of the roofing material above this attic space. Default numbers will be supplied automatically, so don't change these settings unless you plan to specify a cool roof as an improvement.

atticRoofEmissivityImproved.description: Enter the Thermal Emittance or Emissivity of the roofing material above this attic space. Default numbers will be supplied automatically, so don't change these settings unless you plan to specify a cool roof as an improvement.

atticCoolRoof.description: Choose yes if you would like to specify characteristics of a cool roof (such as Whole Home Energy Solutions Qualified roof products) for either the base or improved home. Default numbers will be supplied automatically. Most contractors will not use this option unless they are actually planning to replace a roof as part of an improvement package.

atticCoolRoofImproved.description: Choose yes if you would like to specify characteristics of a cool roof (such as Whole Home Energy Solutions Qualified roof products) for either the base or improved home. Default numbers will be supplied automatically. Most contractors will not use this option unless they are actually planning to replace a roof as part of an improvement package.

atticKneeWallInsulationType.description: Select the type of insulation that is installed in the knee wall cavity. If more than one type of insulation is installed, select the type that covers that largest area.

atticKneeWallInsulationTypeImproved.description: Select the type of insulation that is installed in the knee wall cavity. If more than one type of insulation is installed, select the type that covers that largest area.

atticKneeWallContinuousInsulationType.description: Select the type of continuous insulation that is installed on the knee wall. If more than one type of insulation is installed, select the type that covers that largest area.

atticKneeWallContinuousInsulationTypeImp.description: Select the type of continuous insulation that is installed on the knee wall. If more than one type of insulation is installed, select the type that covers that largest area.

atticRoofType.description: Select the type of roofing material for this roof section.

atticRoofColor.description: Select the color of roofing material for this roof section.

uuid.description: This is a universally unique ID for this item (UUID).

clothesDryerFuelType.description: Select the type of clothes dryer used in the home. If the home has more than one clothes dryer, select the one that is used most often.

clothesDryerFuelTypeImproved.description: Select the type of clothes dryer used in the home. If the home has more than one clothes dryer, select the one that is used most often.

clothesDryerHeatPump.description: Select yes if this a ventless/heat pump dryer.

clothesDryerHeatPumpImproved.description: Select yes if this a ventless/heat pump dryer.

clothesDryerEfficiency.description: Enter the rated Combined Energy Factor for the dryer as specified in the Energy Star website. If only Energy Factor is available, then use CEF=EF/1.15.

clothesDryerEfficiencyImproved.description: Enter the rated Combined Energy Factor for the dryer as specified in the Energy Star website. If only Energy Factor is available, then use CEF=EF/1.15.

clothesDryerManufacturer.description: Enter the manufacturer of the Clothes Dryer.

clothesDryerManufacturerImproved.description: Enter the manufacturer of the Clothes Dryer.

clothesDryerModel.description: Enter the model of the Clothes Dryer.

clothesDryerModelImproved.description: Enter the model of the Clothes Dryer.

clothesDryerSerialNumber.description: Enter the serial number of the Clothes Dryer.

clothesDryerSerialNumberImproved.description: Enter the serial number of the Clothes Dryer.

uuid.description: This is a universally unique ID for this item (UUID).

concernSummary.description: Type a short sentence that details a concern from the homeowner. This could be regarding comfort, health and safety, energy savings, aesthetics, noise, etc. There is no right or wrong answer.

concernDetail.example: Furnace needs to be replaced for additional comfort and health & safety issues.
concernDetail.description: Type a more detailed description of this concern from the homeowner.

uuid.description: This is a universally unique ID for this item (UUID).

dhwAge.description: Select the age in years of the hot water system that stores or delivers the domestic hot water for the home. If more than one type of system is used for hot water in the home, select the age for the one that delivers the most gallons of hot water to the home.

dhwLocation.description: Select the where the hot water system that stores or delivers the domestic hot water for the home is located. If more than one type of system is used for hot water in the home, select the location for the one that delivers the most gallons of hot water to the home.

dhwTemperatureSettings.description: Select the temperature setting for the hot water system that stores or delivers the domestic hot water for the home. If more than one type of system is used for hot water in the home, select the temperature setting for the one that delivers the most gallons of hot water to the home.

dhwEnergyFactor.description: Enter the rated Energy Factor of the water heater without a decimal. Sometimes, the Energy Factor of a water heater is expressed as 0.56 or 0.82. Enter it here as 56 or 82. The Min and Max values change based on the type of water heater. Energy Factors for most equipment can be found at ahridirectory.org.

dhwEnergyFactorImproved.description: Enter the rated Energy Factor of the water heater without a decimal. Sometimes, the Energy Factor of a water heater is expressed as 0.56 or 0.82. Enter it here as 56 or 82. The Min and Max values change based on the type of water heater. Energy Factors for most equipment can be found at ahridirectory.org.

dhwUnifiedEnergyFactor.description: Enter the rated Uniform Energy Factor of the water heater without a decimal. Sometimes, the Uniform Energy Factor of a water heater is expressed as 0.56 or 0.82. Enter it here as 56 or 82. The Min and Max values change based on the type of water heater. Energy Factors for most equipment can be found at ahridirectory.org.

dhwUnifiedEnergyFactorImproved.description: Enter the rated Uniform Energy Factor of the water heater without a decimal. Sometimes, the Uniform Energy Factor of a water heater is expressed as 0.56 or 0.82. Enter it here as 56 or 82. The Min and Max values change based on the type of water heater. Energy Factors for most equipment can be found at ahridirectory.org.

dhwTemp.description: Enter the temperature of the water produced by the water heating system.

dhwTempImproved.description: Enter the temperature of the water produced by the water heating system.

dhwManufacturer.description: Enter the Manufacturer of the water heater.

dhwManufacturerImproved.description: Enter the Manufacturer of the water heater.

dhwModel.description: Enter the Model number of the water heater.

dhwModelImproved.description: Enter the Model number of the water heater.

dhwModelYear.description: Enter the model year of the water heater.

dhwModelYearImproved.description: Enter the model year of the water heater.

dhwType2.description: Enter the type of water heater installed (BASE) or to be installed (IMPROVED).

dhwType2Improved.description: Enter the type of water heater installed (BASE) or to be installed (IMPROVED).

dhwSidearmBoiler.description: Choose the boiler from the HVAC section that this indirect water heater tank is attached to.

dhwSidearmBoilerImproved.description: Choose the boiler from the HVAC section that this indirect water heater tank is attached to.

dhwSidearmStandbyLoss.description: Enter the temperature of the water produced by the water heating system.

dhwSidearmStandbyLossImproved.description: Enter the temperature of the water produced by the water heating system.

dhwPercentLoad.description: Percent of the annual DHW load met by this system. Both systems must add up to 100%.

dhwPercentLoadImproved.description: Percent of the annual DHW load met by this system. Both systems must add up to 100%.

dhwEnergyStar.description: Select Yes if this Water Heater is ENERGY STAR Certified.

dhwEnergyStarImproved.description: Select Yes if this Water Heater is ENERGY STAR Certified.

dhwHeatingCapacity.description: Enter the rated input capacity of the water heater in BTU/hr.

dhwHeatingCapacityImproved.description: Enter the rated input capacity of the water heater in BTU/hr.

dhwTankSize.description: Enter the tank size the water heater in gallons.

dhwTankSizeImproved.description: Enter the tank size the water heater in gallons.

dhwRecoveryEfficiency.description: Enter the system recovery efficiency of the water heater. This field is optional for modeling but is required for some utility programs such as NYSERDA.

dhwRecoveryEfficiencyImproved.description: Enter the system recovery efficiency of the water heater. This field is optional for modeling but is required for some utility programs such as NYSERDA.

dhwFuel2.description: Select the type of energy source used to provide domestic hot water for the home. Domestic hot water is typically the hot water that comes from the faucets, showers, bathtubs, washing machines and dishwashers in the home. If more than one type of energy source is used to provide hot water, select the type that provides the most gallons of hot water.

dhwFuel2Improved.description: Select the type of energy source used to provide domestic hot water for the home. Domestic hot water is typically the hot water that comes from the faucets, showers, bathtubs, washing machines and dishwashers in the home. If more than one type of energy source is used to provide hot water, select the type that provides the most gallons of hot water.

dhwTankWrap.description: Select Yes if tank wrap has been applied to this water heater.

dhwTankWrapImproved.description: Select Yes if tank wrap has been applied to this water heater.

dhwTankWrapRValue.description: Enter the R Value of the tank wrap that has been applied to this water heater.

hesDhwEnergyFactor.description: The efficiency of a storage water heater is indicated by its Energy Factor (EF). If you know or have a good idea what the EF is, (check the EnergyGuide label on the water heater) enter it here, otherwise enter an installed year. This number should be entered as a decimal (e.g. 0.55). The allowed range is 0.45 to 4.0. Combustion water heaters are typically between 0.5 and 0.65. Electric are typically between 0.8 and 0.95. Heat pump water heaters may be as high as 4.

hvacSystemName.description: Give this system a name that you can use to easily differentiate it from the others like: Old Heat Pump or Basement Furnace.

enum: Deep Loop Ground Source Heat Pump (shared ducts)
Open Loop Ground Source Heat Pump (shared ducts)
Shallow Loop Ground Source Heat Pump (shared ducts)
hvacSystemEquipmentType.description: Select which type of HVAC system you'd like to describe. This list shows every possible type of heating or cooling system (or combination thereof) that is available in our software. Pay attention to the “shared ducts” vs. “standalone ducts” component. Note: this is very similar to the “Both” option that was available in version 4.

enum: Keep an existing system as is
hvacUpgradeAction.description:
Choose an action that you plan to take for this HVAC system.**Replace with a newer model:**  
Replace the existing equipment with a new version of the same thing. Eg. replace a 78 AFUE furnace with a new 98 AFUE furnace  
**Keep an existing system as is:**  
Do nothing to a system or just change the percent of total load that that old system covers in the house.  
**Remove a system permanently:**  
Rip out an entire system and replace it with something completely new, like going from a direct heater and room AC to a central heat pump. In this instance you will have a system that is described on the base side, and the improved side will be blank.  
**Install a new non-existing system:**  
A brand new system that replaces the system that was removed when you chose: Remove a system permanently.

hvacHeatingEnergySource.description: This is the primary fuel source. It is used to set prices and energy content.

hvacHeatingEnergySourceImproved.description: This is the primary fuel source. It is used to set prices and energy content.

hvacHeatingIsCondensing.description: Is this furnace or boiler a condensing unit? If you see PVC pipes for the combustion venting as opposed to a gravity vent, then this unit is likely a condensing unit that runs at 90 AFUE or higher. Setting this to true will set the default a calibrated number between 90 and 96 AFUE automatically.

hvacPercentOfTotalHeatingLoad.description: Enter the proportion of the total load for heating or cooling that this system represents. The combined heating load percentage for all heating systems specified must add up to 100%.

hvacPercentOfTotalHeatingLoadImproved.description: Enter the proportion of the total load for heating or cooling that this system represents. The combined heating load percentage for all heating systems specified must add up to 100%.

hvacHeatingCapacity.description:
Enter the output capacity of the heating equipment in (Btu/hr).  
  
**NOTE:** This has changed since v3 of Snugg Pro. v4 and later requires the number be in Btu/hr, not kBtu/hr. All v3 jobs that were migrated to v4 have been converted to the proper number by multiplying it by 1000.

hvacDuctLocation.description: Select the location of the duct work for this system.

hvacDuctLocationImproved.description: Select the location of the duct work for this system.

hvacDuctInsulation.description: Select the type of insulation installed over the duct work for this system. Note: If ducts are covered wholly or partially by incidental insulation present in the cavity in which they are installed, then select the insulation type that most closely matches the average R-value of the insulation. If you know the actual duct insulation R-value, choose Measured (R-Value) and enter the data in the field below.

hvacDuctInsulationImproved.description: Select the type of insulation installed over the duct work for this system. Note: If ducts are covered wholly or partially by incidental insulation present in the cavity in which they are installed, then select the insulation type that most closely matches the average R-value of the insulation. If you know the actual duct insulation R-value, choose Measured (R-Value) and enter the data in the field below.

hvacDuctLeakageValue.description: If you perform a duct blaster test, please enter the tested leakage to outside at 25 Pa per RESNET Standard Section 803.7.

hvacDuctLeakageValueImproved.description: If you perform a duct blaster test, please enter the tested leakage to outside at 25 Pa per RESNET Standard Section 803.7.

hvacDuctEfficiency.description:
This is the overall delivery system efficiency for this distribution system. It is calculated based on the above duct leakage, location, and insulation values and is not editable. If you have chosen Intentionally Conditioned Space for the Duct Location, then the Duct Efficiency will always be 100% as the lost energy from the ducts is only lost into the building envelope and not to the outside.  
  
Also, if you have set the % of Total Load for this heating or cooling system to 0% (necessary when creating a brand new heating or cooling system or removing one), you will see the Duct Efficiency at 100%, since there is zero loss on a system that doesn't exist.

hvacDuctEfficiencyImproved.description:
This is the overall delivery system efficiency for this distribution system. It is calculated based on the above duct leakage, location, and insulation values and is not editable. If you have chosen Intentionally Conditioned Space for the Duct Location, then the Duct Efficiency will always be 100% as the lost energy from the ducts is only lost into the building envelope and not to the outside.  
  
Also, if you have set the % of Total Load for this heating or cooling system to 0% (necessary when creating a brand new heating or cooling system or removing one), you will see the Duct Efficiency at 100%, since there is zero loss on a system that doesn't exist.

hvacPercentOfTotalCoolingLoad.description: Enter the proportion of the total load for heating or cooling that this system represents. The combined cooling load percentage for all cooling systems specified DOES NOT need to add up to 100%. If you set the cooling load to 70% and don't add any other cooling system, then the model assumes you're not conditioning 30% of the home's cooling load.

hvacPercentOfTotalCoolingLoadImproved.description: Enter the proportion of the total load for heating or cooling that this system represents. The combined cooling load percentage for all cooling systems specified DOES NOT need to add up to 100%. If you set the cooling load to 70% and don't add any other cooling system, then the model assumes you're not conditioning 30% of the home's cooling load.

**NOTE:** This has changed since v3 of Snugg Pro. v4 and later requires the number be in Btu/hr, not kBtu/hr. All v3 jobs that were migrated to v4 have been converted to the proper number by multiplying it by 1000.

hvacCoolingSystemEfficiency.description:
The Seasonal Energy Efficiency Ratio (SEER) is the average annual cooling efficiency of an air-conditioning or heat pump system as a weighted average of EERs over a range of rate outside air conditions following a standard test method. It includes energy of auxiliary systems such as the indoor and outdoor fans. Units: Btu/Wh.  
  
The Energy Efficiency Ratio (EER) is the measurement of the cooling capacity for a unit (in Btu/hour) divided by electrical energy it uses (in watts) at a specific temperature of 95ºF. Units: Btu/Wh. EER is only used for Room AC units.  
  
**Note:**  
Edit Base fields in the Details section only if you're confident of the actual value that is entered. The modeling engine utilizes utility data and input form data to determine the "effective" values on the Base side.  
  
Edit Improved fields in the Details section with the details of what you recommend be installed in the house. These values are only used if the item is Recommended. Default values are supplied by the modeling engine, but can be overridden.

The Energy Efficiency Ratio (EER) is the measurement of the cooling capacity for a unit (in Btu/hour) divided by electrical energy it uses (in watts) at a specific temperature of 95ºF. Units: Btu/Wh. EER is only used for Room AC units.

hvacHeatingSystemEfficiency.description:
Annual Fuel Utilization Efficiency (AFUE) is a percentage representing the ratio of heat energy units provided to the total energy value of fuel consumed in identical units. It is most commonly used for combustion based heating as well as electric resistance heating.  
  
Heating Seasonal Performance Factor (HSPF) is the ratio of Btus of heat energy provided to the watt-hours of electricity consumed. It is most commonly used for air and ground source heat pumps.  
  
**Note:**  
Edit Base fields in the Details section only if you're confident of the actual value that is entered. The modeling engine utilizes utility data and input form data to determine the "effective" values on the Base side.  
  
Edit Improved fields in the Details section with the details of what you recommend be installed in the house. These values are only used if the item is Recommended. Default values are supplied by the modeling engine, but can be overridden.

vaultModeledVaultArea.description:
This field will be automatically calculated based on the data you entered in the input form for Conditioned Area and Number of Stories. This is the actual surface area of the vaulted ceiling. It includes a default roof pitch of 5/12. When you override this number, be sure to include the complete sqft of the vault that's covering conditioned space.  
  
This field designates the interior surface area of the vaulted ceiling. On the improved side, this is the new total sqft of the vaulted ceiling. Increase this number if you're switching the attic from unconditioned to a conditioned space.  
  
For instance, if the original house had 1000 sqft of attic space and you converted it to a fully conditioned attic by spray foaming the roof deck and rafters, then set the base side to 1000 sqft and the improved side to 0. Then create a vaulted ceiling that is 0 on the base side and 1118 sqft on the improved side (adding 118 sqft for a 6/12 roof pitch).

vaultModeledVaultAreaImproved.description:
This field will be automatically calculated based on the data you entered in the input form for Conditioned Area and Number of Stories. This is the actual surface area of the vaulted ceiling. It includes a default roof pitch of 5/12. When you override this number, be sure to include the complete sqft of the vault that's covering conditioned space.  
  
This field designates the interior surface area of the vaulted ceiling. On the improved side, this is the new total sqft of the vaulted ceiling. Increase this number if you're switching the attic from unconditioned to a conditioned space.  
  
For instance, if the original house had 1000 sqft of attic space and you converted it to a fully conditioned attic by spray foaming the roof deck and rafters, then set the base side to 1000 sqft and the improved side to 0. Then create a vaulted ceiling that is 0 on the base side and 1118 sqft on the improved side (adding 118 sqft for a 6/12 roof pitch).

vaultCavityInsulation.description:

**Note:**

vaultAssemblyRValue.description: This is the total assembly R-value for the entire vaulted ceiling system. This field is not editable since it is calculated from the other fields.

vault.description:
Is there insulation in the vaulted ceiling cavities of this home? If so, how good is it? If unsure, please leave blank.  
  
**Well**: All cavities contain insulation at full or near full depth with gaps and voids not exceeding 5%.  
  
**Poor**: Majority of cavities contain insulation, but one or more of the following is suspected: low density, low depth, poor installation.  
  
**Yes**: You can confirm that insulation exists, but are unsure of the installation quality.  
  
**No** - No insulation value is applied to cavities.

vaultPercent.description: Enter the % of the total footprint that is attributed to each attic or vault area.

vaultRoofAbsorptance.description: Enter the Solar Absorptance of the roofing material above this vaulted ceiling. Solar Absorptance is equal to 1 - Solar Reflectance. For example, if the ENERGY STAR website gives a Initial Solar Reflectance of 0.27, enter 0.73. Default numbers will be supplied automatically, so don't change these settings unless you plan to specify a cool roof as an improvement.

vaultRoofAbsorptanceImproved.description: Enter the Solar Absorptance of the roofing material above this vaulted ceiling. Solar Absorptance is equal to 1 - Solar Reflectance. For example, if the ENERGY STAR website gives a Initial Solar Reflectance of 0.27, enter 0.73. Default numbers will be supplied automatically, so don't change these settings unless you plan to specify a cool roof as an improvement.

vaultRoofEmissivity.description: Enter the Thermal Emittance or Emissivity of the roofing material above this vaulted ceiling. Default numbers will be supplied automatically, so don't change these settings unless you plan to specify a cool roof as an improvement.

vaultRoofEmissivityImproved.description: Enter the Thermal Emittance or Emissivity of the roofing material above this vaulted ceiling. Default numbers will be supplied automatically, so don't change these settings unless you plan to specify a cool roof as an improvement.

vaultCoolRoof.description: Choose yes if you would like to specify characteristics of a cool roof (such as Whole Home Energy Solutions Qualified roof products) for either the base or improved home. Default numbers will be supplied automatically. Most contractors will not use this option unless they are actually planning to replace a roof as part of an improvement package.

vaultCoolRoofImproved.description: Choose yes if you would like to specify characteristics of a cool roof (such as Whole Home Energy Solutions Qualified roof products) for either the base or improved home. Default numbers will be supplied automatically. Most contractors will not use this option unless they are actually planning to replace a roof as part of an improvement package.

wallAssemblyRValue.description: This is the total assembly R-value for the entire basement wall system. We take the data from the Cavity and Continuous R-value fields and add factors for wood framing, drywall, air film, etc. to calculate the overall assembly R-value. This field is not editable since it is calculated from the other fields.

wallAssemblyRValueImproved.description: This is the total assembly R-value for the entire basement wall system. We take the data from the Cavity and Continuous R-value fields and add factors for wood framing, drywall, air film, etc. to calculate the overall assembly R-value. This field is not editable since it is calculated from the other fields.

wallModeledWallArea.description:
This field will be automatically calculated based on the data you entered in the input form for House Length, House Width, and Average Wall Height.  
  
This field designates the total gross exterior wall area, including all window and door openings. It does not include walls shared in common with other conditioned dwelling units. An additional 1' of wall height is automatically included for every story beyond the first to account for rim joists. First floor (foundation) rim joists are included separately in the basement or crawl space wall areas.  
  
The BASE area designates the sqft of this wall system on the house. The IMPROVED area is the actual sqft of surface area in the new home. Changing the IMPROVED area will reduce or increase the sqft of wall area in the home. If you want to improve only a portion of the walls, then you must build a second wall system where you improve one of the systems, but not the other.

wallModeledWallAreaImproved.description:
This field will be automatically calculated based on the data you entered in the input form for House Length, House Width, and Average Wall Height.  
  
This field designates the total gross exterior wall area, including all window and door openings. It does not include walls shared in common with other conditioned dwelling units. An additional 1' of wall height is automatically included for every story beyond the first to account for rim joists. First floor (foundation) rim joists are included separately in the basement or crawl space wall areas.  
  
The BASE area designates the sqft of this wall system on the house. The IMPROVED area is the actual sqft of surface area in the new home. Changing the IMPROVED area will reduce or increase the sqft of wall area in the home. If you want to improve only a portion of the walls, then you must build a second wall system where you improve one of the systems, but not the other.

wallSystemPercentOfTotal.description: If there is more than one wall system type, enter the % of all walls that this system represents. Both wall systems must add up to 100%. If there is only one type of wall system in the house, then enter 100%.

windowEnergyStar.description: Select Yes if this window system is ENERGY STAR Certified.

windowEnergyStarImproved.description: Select Yes if this window system is ENERGY STAR Certified.

windowHesSpecs.description: This field is used for Snugg Score jobs, not audits. If set to Yes, the windows U-Value and Solar Heat Gain Coefficient must be specified. If set to No, the window type and frame but be specified.

uuid.description: This is a universally unique ID for this item (UUID).

status.description:
**Recommend** a measure to feature it in the report's upgrade details section. This measure will factor into the job's total cost, savings and SIR.

**Note** a measure to list it in the report as 'Additional Notes'. Noted measures do not count towards the job's total cost, savings and SIR.

**Decline** a measure to hide it altogether report. Declined measures do not count towards the job's total cost, savings and SIR.

  
Field options:  
0 → Default System Recommend  
1 → Recommend  
2 → Note  
3 → Decline

title.description: The title will appear throughout the audit report. When appropriate, consider wording this as an actionable sentence. For instance, instead of 'Programmable Thermostat', you could write 'Program your thermostat'.

measureCode.description: Used to identify a measure with a custom code.

homeownerNotes.description: Add a note to the homeowner about this recommendation

contractorNotes.description: This field is intended to communicate details with other professionals. It can be turned on or off in the report so you can chose to hide technical jargon or other information when presenting to the homeowner.

whyItMatters.description: Best kept short and concise, this field is largely educational and appears in the sidebar of the recommendation.

id.description: The unique Snugg Pro job identifier. Do not specify this when creating a job via API

fromTemplateId.description: Specify a job template ID if you'd like to use a template for this new job.

renterOwner.description:
Does the customer own or rent this property.  
Field options:  
0 → Renter  
1 → Owner

serviceTime.description: Time at which the appointment will take place. Format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS

email.description: The email address of the homeowner (or tenant).

homePhone.description: Phone number of the home in this format (555) 555-5555.

address1.description: First line of the home address.

address2.description: Second line of the home address.

accountId.description: Unique identifier of the Snugg Pro user who owns this job.

stageId.description:
Current stage ID of the job.  
Field options:  
1 → Lead  
2 → Audit  
3 → Bid Proposed  
4 → Bid Approved  
5 → Retrofit In Progress  
6 → Retrofit Complete  
7 → QA  
8 → Uncategorized  
9 → Archived Won  
10 → Archived Lost

jobType.description:
Job Type ID of the job.  
Field options:  
1 → Audit  
2 → Score

companyId.description: Unique identifier of the Snugg Pro company who owns the job.

programId.description: Unique identifier for the program that this job is in. Pass the value "1" for no program

altId.description: Store your own alternate job ID. This will not affect the Snugg Pro assigned Job ID.

buildingId.description: Store an arbitrary building ID here if needed. When present, this value will be inserted in the following HPXML node: Building/extension/HESExternalID. This value is used as the external building ID when getting a DOE Home Energy Score.

propertyOwnerAddress1.description: First line of the property owner's address. This is useful if the property is not owner occupied or if the owner wants to list a different address for contact purposes).

propertyOwnerAddress2.description: Second line of the property owner's address. This is useful if the property is not owner occupied or if the owner wants to list a different address for contact purposes).

propertyOwnerEmail.description: The email address of the property owner. Use this field if the property is tenant-occupied and the job's regular email field is populated with the tenant's email address.

propertyOwnerPhone.description: Phone number of the property owner in this format (555) 555-5555. Use this field if the property is tenant-occupied and the regular phone number field is populated with the tenant's phone number.

propertyOwnerCity.description: City of the property owner. Use this field if home is tenant-occupied and you want to capture the homeowner's address.

propertyOwnerState.description: State of the property owner. Use this field if home is tenant-occupied and you want to capture the homeowner's address.

propertyOwnerZip.description: Zip code of the property owner. Use this field if home is tenant-occupied and you want to capture the homeowner's address.

auditType.description: This field is only enabled for select programs.

doeWap.description: Mark yes if this job is an official DOE weatherization job.

hasCalculated.description: Indicates whether a job has ever been calculated

hasUnmodeledChanges.description: Indicates whether changes that affect modelling were made since the last time the job was modelled.

completionDate.description: This field can appear on the certificate of completion. It can be used to indicate the job's completion or installation date.

firstModeledAt.description: This field is set on the first job model.

hasOutdatedHpxml.description: Indicates whether changes that affect HPXML were made since the last time the job was modelled.

note.description: Note to assignee or assigned user.

useStrictBpi2400Calibration.description:
Get enhanced calibration for BPI 2400 calibration.  
Field options:  
1 → Yes  
0 → No

householdIncomeTier.description: Household income tier for the job.

id.description: This is the unique Snugg Pro ID assigned to the user.

email.description: This is the user's login email address, which may be different from the address displayed in the report.

id.description: This is the unique Snugg Pro ID assigned to the company.

name.description: Enter the depth of all installed attic or ceiling insulation. If the insulation is not evenly distributed, estimate an average depth for the area. If the insulation is evenly distributed but has different depths installed in different area, use the depth of insulation that covers the largest area. If there is no attic and instead vaulted ceilings, enter an estimate of the thickness of insulation in the vaulted cavity.

paybackYears.description: The fist year with non-negative cumulative cash flow which includes energy escalation.

sir.description: The Savings to Investment Ratio. If the SIR is 1 or greater, then the energy savings from the item will pay for itself before it needs to be replaced again.

mirr.description: The Modified Internal Rate of Return. This is the return on investment. Roughly speaking, if you invested the same amount of money for this project into a bank account, your equivalent interest rate from all of the energy savings would be the MIRR.

cazAmbientCo.description: Ambient Carbon Monoxide in Parts Per Million. Should not exceed 35 ppm per BPI Protocol.

cazAmbientCoImproved.description: Ambient Carbon Monoxide in Parts Per Million. Should not exceed 35 ppm per BPI Protocol.

cazPoorCaseTest.description: The poor case CAZ depressurization test is configured by determining the largest combustion appliance zone depressurization attainable at the time of testing due to the combined effects of door position, exhaust appliance operation, and air handler fan operation. A base pressure must be measured with all fans off and doors open. The poor case CAZ depressurization measurement is the pressure difference between the largest depressurization attained at the time of testing and the base pressure.

cazPoorCaseTestImproved.description: The poor case CAZ depressurization test is configured by determining the largest combustion appliance zone depressurization attainable at the time of testing due to the combined effects of door position, exhaust appliance operation, and air handler fan operation. A base pressure must be measured with all fans off and doors open. The poor case CAZ depressurization measurement is the pressure difference between the largest depressurization attained at the time of testing and the base pressure.

If you performed an actual test and it passed, then choose "Passed", if the test failed, then choose "Failed". If you did not perform this test, but suspect there might be issues or have notes to provide the homeowner or contractor, then choose "Warning". If you did not perform this test and do not want the test information to show up on the homeowner report, then choose "Not Tested".
